1.1.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of my spiritual master. I offer my respectful obeisances to Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, who appeared in this world with Nityananda Prabhu and is surrounded by His devotees.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to whom does the author first offer his respectful obeisances in the opening verse?
questions.explanation: The text begins with an offering of obeisances to the lotus feet of the author's spiritual master.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides his devotees, who is Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu explicitly mentioned as appearing with?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu appeared in this world with Nityananda Prabhu.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the internal nature of the gathering around Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu described?
options: He is surrounded by His devotees
He is surrounded by the gopis
He is surrounded by the Pandavas
He is surrounded by the residents of Navadvipa
questions.correct_answer: He is surrounded by His devotees
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu is surrounded by His devotees.

1.2.verse_text: I offer my respectful obeisances to the two lotus feet Srimati Radharani. I offer my respectful obeisances Lord Nandanandana, who is surrounded by the gopis and enchants the minds of the residents of Vrndavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose two lotus feet are described as the primary object of the author's respectful obeisances?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states the author offers his respectful obeisances to the two lotus feet of Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by whom is Lord Nandanandana surrounded?
questions.explanation: The text describes Lord Nandanandana as being surrounded by the gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what effect does Lord Nandanandana have on the minds of the residents of Vrndavana?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Lord Nandanandana as the one who enchants the minds of the residents of Vrndavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which divine name is used to address Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse refers to Lord Krsna specifically as Lord Nandanandana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is described as enchanting the minds of the residents of Vrndavana?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Lord Nandanandana is the one who enchants the minds of the residents of Vrndavana.

1.3.verse_text: The celebrated personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana are briefly but truthfully described with great delight in both Vedic literatures and oral tradition. This book will also describe them.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary motivation stated for describing the personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana?
options: To fulfill the desires of the king of Mathura
To describe them with great delight
To challenge the scholars of the Puranas
To explain the grammar of the Vedas
questions.correct_answer: To describe them with great delight
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana are described with great delight.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two sources of knowledge confirm the truthfulness of the descriptions of the Divine Couple's associates?
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that the associates are described truthfully in both Vedic literatures and oral tradition.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana described in terms of the length or style of the account?
questions.explanation: The text characterizes the description of the associates as being brief but truthful.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the nature of the figures being described in the book?
options: The personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana
The demigods ruling the heavenly planets
questions.correct_answer: The personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana
questions.explanation: The text identifies the subjects of the descriptions as the celebrated personal associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana.

1.4-5.verse_text: These associates of the Lord are described by the residents of Mathura-mandala, by the various books written by devotees, various Vedic literatures such as the Puranas and Agamas and by the great devotees and saintly persons. For the satisfaction of my dear friends, these associates of the Lord will now be briefly describing in writing, following the previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love of God.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, for whose satisfaction does the author choose to briefly describe the associates of the Lord?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the descriptions are being written for the satisfaction of the author's dear friends.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific categories of literary and traditional authority are cited as sources for describing the Lord's associates?
options: Puranas, Agamas, and books by devotees
questions.correct_answer: Puranas, Agamas, and books by devotees
questions.explanation: The text mentions the associates are described by books written by devotees and Vedic literatures such as the Puranas and Agamas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the foundational path being followed when the author documents the descriptions of the associates?
options: The path of ecstatic love of God
The path of rigorous ascetic meditation
The path of traditional ritualistic worship
questions.correct_answer: The path of ecstatic love of God
questions.explanation: The text confirms the author is writing following the previous authorities in the path of ecstatic love of God.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the books written by devotees, who else is cited as describing the associates of the Lord?
questions.explanation: The text states that the associates are described by great devotees and saintly persons in addition to the residents of Mathura-mandala.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what approach is being taken regarding the length and method of the upcoming descriptions?
options: They will be brief and follow previous authorities
They will be exhaustive and based on personal visions
They will be brief and based on newly revealed knowledge
They will be exhaustive and based on oral tradition alone
questions.correct_answer: They will be brief and follow previous authorities
questions.explanation: The author specifies that the associates will now be briefly described in writing, following the previous authorities.

1.6.verse_text: The associates of Krsna who reside in Vrajabhumi may be divided into three groups : 1. protectors of domestic animals, 2. brahmanas, 3. others.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, into how many distinct groups are the associates of Lord Krsna who reside in Vrajabhumi divided?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the associates of Krsna residing in Vrajabhumi are divided into three groups.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is categorized as one of the three primary groups of residents in Vrajabhumi?
options: The demigods of the heavenly planets
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the protectors of domestic animals as the first of the three groups of Vraja residents.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the protectors of domestic animals and the brahmanas, what is the third category of residents in Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The text lists the three groups as: 1. protectors of domestic animals, 2. brahmanas, and 3. others.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group is mentioned specifically alongside the 'protectors of domestic animals' and 'others' as inhabitants of Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly lists the brahmanas as the second group among the associates of Krsna in Vrajabhumi.

1.7.verse_text: The Protectors of Domestic Animals: These are descendants of the Yadu dynasty and they may be divided into three groups : 1. vaisyas, 2. abhiras, 3. gurjaras.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the protectors of domestic animals in Vrajabhumi are described as descendants of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the protectors of domestic animals are descendants of the Yadu dynasty.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the group known as the protectors of domestic animals is divided into how many sub-groups?
questions.explanation: The text states that the protectors of domestic animals may be divided into three groups.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the three specific groups of protectors of domestic animals?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the three groups of protectors of domestic animals as vaisyas, abhiras, and gurjaras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which category of residents in Vraja are specifically identified by the name gurjaras?
questions.explanation: The text classifies the gurjaras as one of the three groups constituting the protectors of domestic animals.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the group referred to as abhiras is counted under which primary classification of Vraja residents?
questions.explanation: The text defines the abhiras as one of the three groups belonging to the protectors of domestic animals.

1.8.verse_text: The Vaisyas: For the most part the most important vaisyas earn their livelihood by protecting cows. Some of the vaisyas are born from lower-caste mothers and they are known as abhiras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary occupation of the most important Vaisyas in Vraja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that for the most part, the most important vaisyas earn their livelihood by protecting cows.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the origin mentioned for the group of cowherds known as Abhiras?
options: They are born from lower-caste mothers
They are descendants of the royal Yadu line
They are the sons of the local Brahmin priests
They are celestial beings taking birth in Vraja
questions.correct_answer: They are born from lower-caste mothers
questions.explanation: The verse states that some of the vaisyas are born from lower-caste mothers and are known as abhiras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which classification do the individuals known as Abhiras belong to as a subgroup?
questions.explanation: The verse classifies the Abhiras as a subset of the vaisya category, specifically those born from lower-caste mothers.

1.9.verse_text: The Abhiras: The abhiras are those who have vaisya fathers and sudra mothers. A little inferior to the vaisyas, who are engaged in cow protection, the abhiras earn their livelihood by protecting buffalo. They are known by various names, such as ghosa.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the occupations of the parents of those cowherds known as the abhiras?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the abhiras are born from fathers who are vaisyas and mothers who are sudras.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific animals are the abhiras responsible for protecting to earn their livelihood?
questions.explanation: The text distinguishes the abhiras from the vaisyas by noting that while vaisyas protect cows, the abhiras earn their livelihood by protecting buffalo.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what other name are the abhiras commonly known?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that the abhiras are known by various names, explicitly citing ghosa as one of them.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the social standing of the abhiras described in relation to the vaisyas?
options: A little inferior to the vaisyas
questions.correct_answer: A little inferior to the vaisyas
questions.explanation: The scripture clearly defines the status of the abhiras as being a little inferior to the vaisyas who are engaged in cow protection.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group is specifically described as being engaged in the protection of cows?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the vaisyas as the group primarily engaged in the livelihood of protecting cows.

1.10.verse_text: The Garjaras: A little inferior to the abhiras, the garjaras earn their livelihood by protecting goats and similar animals. Their bodily features are a little chubby and they reside on the outskirts of Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary occupation of the group known as the Garjaras?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the Garjaras earn their livelihood by protecting goats and similar animals.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the Garjaras described in relation to the Abhiras in terms of social standing?
questions.explanation: The text describes the Garjaras as being a little inferior to the Abhiras.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the characteristic bodily appearance of the Garjaras?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the bodily features of the Garjaras are a little chubby.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where do the Garjaras reside within the land of Vraja?
options: Near the bank of the Yamuna
questions.explanation: The text indicates that the Garjaras reside on the outskirts of Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what animals do the Garjaras tend to, distinguishing them from the buffalo-herding Abhiras?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the Garjaras' livelihood specifically through the protection of goats and similar animals.

1.11.verse_text: The Brahmanas: Learned in all the Vedas, the brahmanas engage in performing sacrifices, worshiping the Deity and other brahminical occupations.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary scholarly qualification attributed to the brahmanas of Vrajabhumi?
options: They are learned in all the Vedas
They are expert in milking the surabhi cows
They are masters of all musical instruments
They are skilled in the art of archery
questions.correct_answer: They are learned in all the Vedas
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes the brahmanas as being learned in all the Vedas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the specific occupational duties assigned to the brahmanas living in the land of Vraja?
options: Engaging in various trades and crafts
Performing sacrifices and worshiping the Deity
Tending to the gardens of Govardhana
questions.correct_answer: Performing sacrifices and worshiping the Deity
questions.explanation: The text states that the brahmanas engage in performing sacrifices, worshiping the Deity, and other brahminical occupations.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the general category of occupations mentioned for the brahmanas aside from sacrifice and Deity worship?
questions.explanation: The verse states they engage in performing sacrifices, worshiping the Deity, and other brahminical occupations.

1.12.verse_text: The Bahisthas: The bahisthas earn their livelihood by engaging in various trades and crafts. In this way we have described five kinds of associates (vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas and bahisthas) of Lord Hari in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary means of livelihood attributed to the class of Vraja residents known as the bahisthas?
options: Engaging in various trades and crafts
Herding the transcendental cows of Nanda Maharaja
Performing Vedic sacrifices and Deity worship
Assisting the gopis in their daily household services
questions.correct_answer: Engaging in various trades and crafts
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the bahisthas earn their livelihood by engaging in various trades and crafts.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many specific categories of Lord Hari's associates in Vrajabhumi are summarized at the conclusion of the description of the bahisthas?
questions.explanation: The text concludes its summary by stating there are five kinds of associates: vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas, and bahisthas.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following groups is explicitly listed among the five kinds of associates of Lord Hari in Vrajabhumi alongside the bahisthas?
questions.explanation: The text lists the five groups as vaisyas, abhiras, garjaras, brahmanas, and bahisthas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the bahisthas, which other group mentioned in the list of five associates is tasked with the occupation of Deity worship and Vedic sacrifice?
questions.explanation: The list of five associates includes the brahmanas, who are identified in the broader context as being learned in the Vedas and engaged in sacrifices and Deity worship.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the collective name used for the group whose livelihood consists of engaging in various trades and crafts in Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the bahisthas as the specific group who earn their livelihood through various trades and crafts.

1.13.verse_text: Lord Krsna's associates may also be divided into the following eight groups : 1. worshipable superiors, 2. relatives who are on the same level (such as brothers and sisters) 3. gopi messengers, 4. servants, 5. craftsmen, 6. maidservants, 7. contemporary cowherd friends and 8. dear gopi friends. Included among the worshipable superiors are the brothers and similar relatives of Nanda Maharaja, Nanda's contemporary friends, servants, other associates and the elderly gopis. Krsna is always respectful to these superiors.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many distinct groups are Lord Krsna's associates divided into when considering their specific roles?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly lists eight specific categories of associates, ranging from worshipable superiors to dear gopi friends.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific category includes individuals such as Nanda Maharaja's brothers?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the brothers of Nanda Maharaja as being included among the worshipable superiors to whom Krsna is always respectful.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the elderly gopis, who else is specifically listed as being included among the worshipable superiors?
questions.explanation: The text lists Nanda's contemporary friends, along with brothers, servants, and elderly gopis, as being part of the worshipable superior category.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group is categorized specifically as 'relatives who are on the same level'?
questions.explanation: The text defines the second group of associates as relatives who are on the same level, specifically mentioning brothers and sisters.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does Lord Krsna relate to those who are classified as worshipable superiors?
options: He is always respectful to them
He engages them as his messengers
questions.correct_answer: He is always respectful to them
questions.explanation: The concluding sentence of the verse explicitly states that Krsna is always respectful to these worshipable superiors.

1.14.verse_text: 1. Worshipable Superiors: The worshipable superiors include Krnsa's grandfather, other elderly relatives and also the community of brahmanas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following groups is explicitly mentioned as belonging to the category of Krsna's worshipable superiors?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the worshipable superiors as including Krsna's grandfather, other elderly relatives, and the community of brahmanas.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides elderly relatives and the community of brahmanas, who else is explicitly listed within the group of Krsna's worshipable superiors?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Krsna's grandfather as a primary member of the worshipable superiors category.

1.15-16.verse_text: Krsna's fair-complexion, white-haired and white-attired grandfather is named Parjanya Maharaja, because he is like a great cloud (parjanya) showering the nectar of auspiciousness. He is the best of all the residents of Vraja. Desiring a worthy descendant, he followed the advice of Devarsi Narada and engaged in the worship of Lord Narayana, the husband of Laksmi. As he was worshiping Narayana at Nandisvara-pura, a pleasant voice spoke the following words from the sky.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of Krsna's grandfather, Parjanya Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Parjanya Maharaja has a fair complexion.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which color clothing is Parjanya Maharaja described as wearing?
questions.explanation: The text describes Parjanya Maharaja as white-attired.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why is Parjanya Maharaja compared to a great cloud?
options: Because he showers the nectar of auspiciousness
Because he is dark like a monsoon cloud
Because he lives on the peak of Govardhana
questions.correct_answer: Because he showers the nectar of auspiciousness
questions.explanation: The text explains his name Parjanya is due to him being like a cloud showering the nectar of auspiciousness.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who gave the advice that Parjanya Maharaja followed to obtain a worthy descendant?
questions.explanation: The text notes that Parjanya Maharaja followed the advice of Devarsi Narada to engage in the worship of Lord Narayana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where was Parjanya Maharaja worshiping Lord Narayana when he heard the divine voice from the sky?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Parjanya Maharaja was worshiping Lord Narayana at Nandisvara-pura when the divine voice spoke.

1.17-18.verse_text: "Because of your pious austerities you will obtain five sons. Of these sons the middle one, named Nanda, will be the best. Nanda's glorious son will delight the residents of Vraja. He will defeat all oppositions. Both the demigods and demons will worship Him, touching the jewels of their crowns to His lotus feet."

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many sons was Parjanya Maharaja promised to obtain as a result of his pious austerities?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that as a result of his pious austerities, Parjanya Maharaja would obtain five sons.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of Parjanya Maharaja's sons is described as being the best among them?
questions.explanation: The text identifies that of the five sons obtained by Parjanya Maharaja, the middle one, named Nanda, is the best.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what will Nanda's glorious son do to the residents of Vraja?
options: Rule over them as a king
Teach them the path of liberation
questions.explanation: The text states that Nanda's glorious son will delight the residents of Vraja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, when demigods and demons worship the lotus feet of Nanda's son, what do they touch to His feet?
questions.explanation: The text describes that both demigods and demons will worship Him by touching the jewels of their crowns to His lotus feet.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what will Nanda's glorious son eventually do regarding all oppositions?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Nanda's glorious son will defeat all oppositions.

1.19.verse_text: Hearing this, Parjanya Maharaja became happy and decided to reside at the place. He lived there until he saw the Kesi demon arrive and at that time he became frightened and fled with all his associates to Mahavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which exalted personality felt great happiness after hearing a prophecy regarding his five sons and subsequently decided to reside in a specific place?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Parjanya Maharaja as the one who heard the auspicious prophecy and decided to reside in that location.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what event caused Parjanya Maharaja to become frightened and leave his residence?
options: The arrival of the Kesi demon
The appearance of the Bakasura demon
The disturbance caused by the Putana demon
The sudden arrival of the Aghasura demon
questions.correct_answer: The arrival of the Kesi demon
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Parjanya Maharaja became frightened and fled upon seeing the Kesi demon arrive.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where did Parjanya Maharaja flee to after being frightened by the Kesi demon?
questions.explanation: The verse clearly mentions that Parjanya Maharaja fled with all his associates to Mahavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who accompanied Parjanya Maharaja when he fled to Mahavana due to fear?
options: All the cowherd men of Vraja
questions.explanation: The text notes that Parjanya Maharaja fled to Mahavana along with all his associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the sequence of events regarding Parjanya Maharaja's residence?
options: He lived there until the arrival of the Kesi demon
He lived there until the birth of Krishna
He lived there until the arrival of Akrura
He lived there until the killing of Kamsa
questions.correct_answer: He lived there until the arrival of the Kesi demon
questions.explanation: The text specifies that his residency continued until the specific moment the Kesi demon arrived.

1.20.verse_text: Krsna's grandmother Variyasi is respected in the land of Vraja. She is short in stature and her complexion is the colour of a kusumbha flower. She is attired in green garments and her hair is the color of milk.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
options: The color of a kusumbha flower
The color of a lotus petal
The color of a ripe pomegranate
questions.correct_answer: The color of a kusumbha flower
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Variyasi's complexion is the color of a kusumbha flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Variyasi as being attired in green garments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the physical stature of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Variyasi as being short in stature.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the distinct color of the hair of Krsna's grandmother, Variyasi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Variyasi's hair as being the color of milk.

1.21.verse_text: Parjanya Maharaja's brothers are named Urjanya and Rajanya and his sister is an expert dancer named Suverjana. Her husband is named Gunavira and they live in the town named Surya-kunja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of the two brothers of Parjanya Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Parjanya Maharaja's brothers are named Urjanya and Rajanya.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the sister of Parjanya Maharaja who is renowned as an expert dancer?
questions.explanation: The text describes Parjanya Maharaja's sister as an expert dancer named Suverjana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the husband of Suverjana?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Gunavira as the husband of Suverjana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in which town do Suverjana and her husband Gunavira reside?
questions.explanation: The text states that Suverjana and her husband Gunavira live in the town named Surya-kunja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific talent or quality is attributed to Suverjana?
options: She is a master of cooking
She is an expert in singing
questions.explanation: The text describes Parjanya Maharaja's sister, Suverjana, as being an expert dancer.

1.22-23.verse_text: Krsna's father is Nanda Maharaja. Nanda delights the residents of Vraja and he is worshiped by all the worlds. He has a protuberant belly, his complexion is the colour of sandalwood, he is tall in stature and his garments are the colour of a bandhujiva flower. His beard is a mixture of black and white hairs, like rice and toasted sesame seeds mixed together.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of Nanda Maharaja?
options: The color of a bandhujiva flower
The color of a dark monsoon cloud
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Nanda Maharaja as having a complexion the color of sandalwood.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the comparison used to describe the mixture of black and white hairs in Nanda Maharaja's beard?
options: Rice and toasted sesame seeds mixed together
Stars scattered across a night sky
White clouds passing over a dark mountain
questions.correct_answer: Rice and toasted sesame seeds mixed together
questions.explanation: The text states his beard is a mixture of black and white hairs, like rice and toasted sesame seeds mixed together.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by Nanda Maharaja?
options: The color of a bandhujiva flower
The color of a blue lotus
The color of a yellow campaka flower
The color of a white jasmine blossom
questions.correct_answer: The color of a bandhujiva flower
questions.explanation: The text describes Nanda Maharaja's garments as being the color of a bandhujiva flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which physical feature is noted about Nanda Maharaja's stature and belly?
options: He is tall with a protuberant belly
He is medium-statured with an athletic frame
He is exceptionally tall and very slender
questions.correct_answer: He is tall with a protuberant belly
questions.explanation: The text specifically mentions that Nanda Maharaja has a protuberant belly and is tall in stature.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is Nanda Maharaja's influence upon the residents of Vraja described?

He instructs the residents of Vraja
He delights the residents of Vraja
He is ignored by the residents of Vraja

questions.explanation: The text states that Nanda Maharaja delights the residents of Vraja and is worshiped by all the worlds.

1.24.verse_text: Nanda is the king of the cowherd men, the younger brother of Upananda and the intimate friend of Maharaja Vasudeva. Nanda and his wife Yasoda are the king and queen of Vrajabhumi and the parents of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, of whom is Nanda Maharaja the younger brother?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Nanda is the younger brother of Upananda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is described as the intimate friend of Maharaja Vasudeva?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Nanda Maharaja as the intimate friend of Maharaja Vasudeva.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what title is given to Nanda Maharaja regarding his position among the cowherd men?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly refers to Nanda as the king of the cowherd men.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who, along with Nanda Maharaja, are identified as the king and queen of Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The text declares Nanda and his wife Yasoda as the king and queen of Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are defined as the parents of Lord Krsna in the context of Vrajabhumi's royalty?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Nanda and his wife Yasoda as the parents of Lord Krsna.

1.25.verse_text: Because he possesses the splendor (deva) of great wealth (vasu), Nanda Maharaja is also known as Vasudeva. He is also famous by the name Anakadundubhi and it is known that in his previous birth he was named Drona.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why is Nanda Maharaja also known by the name Vasudeva?
options: Because he is the son of Vasudeva
Because he possesses the splendor of great wealth
Because he resides in the city of Vasudeva
Because he is a devotee of Lord Vasudeva
questions.correct_answer: Because he possesses the splendor of great wealth
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Nanda Maharaja is called Vasudeva because he possesses the splendor of great wealth.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which name is mentioned as another famous title for Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Nanda Maharaja is also famous by the name Anakadundubhi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the name of Nanda Maharaja in his previous birth?
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that it is known that in his previous birth, Nanda Maharaja was named Drona.

1.26.verse_text: These names of Maharaja Nanda are described in the Mathura-mahima section of the Garuda Purana. Nanda's most intimate friend is named Maharaja Vrsabhanu.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which Purana contains the Mathura-mahima section that details the various names of Maharaja Nanda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that the names of Maharaja Nanda are found within the Mathura-mahima section of the Garuda Purana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the most intimate friend of Maharaja Nanda?
questions.explanation: The verse clearly identifies Maharaja Vrsabhanu as being the most intimate friend of Maharaja Nanda.

1.27.verse_text: Krnsa's mother is named Yasoda because she bestows (da) fame (yasah) on the cowherd residents of Vraja. She is like the personification of parental love for Krnsa. Her complexion is dark and her garments are like a rainbow.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why is Krsna's mother known by the name Yasoda?
options: Because she provides shelter to all
Because she bestows fame on the cowherd residents of Vraja
Because she is the queen of the Vraja-vasis
Because she is the daughter of the king of Vraja
questions.correct_answer: Because she bestows fame on the cowherd residents of Vraja
questions.explanation: The text explains that she is named Yasoda because she bestows (da) fame (yasah) on the cowherd residents of Vraja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what unique comparison is used to describe Mother Yasoda's essence?
options: She is the personification of divine knowledge
She is the personification of parental love for Krsna
She is the personification of selfless service
She is the personification of Vraja-bhakti
questions.correct_answer: She is the personification of parental love for Krsna
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that she is like the personification of parental love for Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the bodily complexion of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text describes Mother Yasoda's complexion as dark.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is used to describe the appearance of the garments worn by Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that Mother Yasoda's garments are like a rainbow.

1.28.verse_text: Mother Yasoda's body is of a medium size, neither large nor small. She has long black hair. Her closest friends are Aindavi and Kirtida.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the physical stature of Mother Yasoda described?
options: Of a medium size, neither large nor small
questions.correct_answer: Of a medium size, neither large nor small
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Mother Yasoda's body is of a medium size, being neither large nor small.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the characteristics of Mother Yasoda's hair?
questions.explanation: The text describes Mother Yasoda as having long black hair.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the two closest friends of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text specifically names Aindavi and Kirtida as the closest friends of Mother Yasoda.

1.29.verse_text: Yasoda is the wife of Nanda, the king of Gokula. She is the queen of the cowherd men in Vraja, the friend of Devaki and the mother of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the husband of mother Yasoda and the king of Gokula?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Yasoda is the wife of Nanda, who is the king of Gokula.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the official status of Yasoda among the cowherd community in Vraja?
options: The queen of the cowherd men
The primary advisor to the elders
The manager of the dairy herds
questions.correct_answer: The queen of the cowherd men
questions.explanation: The text describes Yasoda as the queen of the cowherd men in Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the dear friend of mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Yasoda as the friend of Devaki.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what role does mother Yasoda occupy in relation to Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Yasoda specifically as the mother of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which domain does Nanda Maharaja rule over as king?
questions.explanation: The text states that Nanda is the king of Gokula and the husband of Yasoda.

1.30.verse_text: The friendship of Yasoda is described in the following statement of the Adi Purana : "The wife of Maharaja Nanda was known by two names, Yasoda and Devaki. Partly because they share the same name (Devaki) the wife of Maharaja Nanda and the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva were great friends."

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides Yasoda, what other name is associated with the wife of Maharaja Nanda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the wife of Maharaja Nanda was known by two names, Yasoda and Devaki.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is mentioned as the primary reason for the deep friendship between the wife of Maharaja Nanda and the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva?
options: They grew up in the same village
They share the same devotional service
questions.explanation: The text identifies that they were great friends partly because they share the same name, Devaki.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, from which scriptural source is the friendship between Yasoda and the wife of Vasudeva described?
questions.explanation: The text directly cites the Adi Purana as the source for the description of Yasoda's friendship.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva is identified by what name in the context of her friendship with Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text notes that the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva shares the name Devaki with the wife of Maharaja Nanda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the wife of Maharaja Nanda held what position as a result of her being named Devaki?
options: Friend of the wife of Maharaja Vasudeva

questions.explanation: The text explains that the friendship between the wives of Nanda and Vasudeva is linked to their sharing the name Devaki.

1.31.verse_text: Lord Balarama's mother is named Rohini, because she is filled with a constantly rising (arohini) flood of transcendental bliss. Although she dearly loves her son Balarama, she loves Krsna millions of times more.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, for what specific reason is Lord Balarama's mother known by the name Rohini?
options: Because she possesses a glowing golden complexion
Because she is filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
Because she originates from the lineage of Rohini-naksatra
Because she eternally resides in the forest of Rohini
questions.correct_answer: Because she is filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
questions.explanation: The text explains that her name Rohini is derived from the fact that she is filled with an 'arohini' or constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the intensity of mother Rohini's love for her own son Balarama compare to her love for Krsna?
options: She loves Balarama and Krsna equally
She loves Balarama slightly more than Krsna
She loves Krsna millions of times more than she loves Balarama
She loves Balarama exclusively, regarding Krsna as a distant nephew
questions.correct_answer: She loves Krsna millions of times more than she loves Balarama
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that while she dearly loves her son Balarama, her love for Krsna is millions of times greater.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the mother of Lord Balarama?
questions.explanation: The verse directly identifies Rohini as the mother of Lord Balarama.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what unique internal state characterizes the mother of Lord Balarama?
options: A constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
A profound sense of maternal duty towards the Vrajavasis
An eternal yearning for the return of Vasudeva
A deep meditation upon the glories of Vaikuntha
questions.correct_answer: A constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss
questions.explanation: The text attributes the meaning of her name, Rohini, to being filled with a constantly rising flood of transcendental bliss.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, when describing the heart of Mother Rohini, how is her affection for Balarama contrasted with her affection for the Supreme Lord?
options: Her love for Balarama is described as mere duty, while her love for Krsna is bliss
She loves Balarama dearly, yet Krsna receives a greater magnitude of her affection
She loves Balarama with divine wisdom, and Krsna with simple affection
She loves Balarama as the eldest, and Krsna as the youngest
questions.correct_answer: She loves Balarama dearly, yet Krsna receives a greater magnitude of her affection
questions.explanation: The text acknowledges her dear love for Balarama but emphasizes that her love for Krsna is millions of times greater.

1.32.verse_text: Nanda Maharaja's elder brothers are Upananda and Abhinanda and his younger brothers are Sannanda and Nandana. These are the names of Lord Krsna's parental uncles.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of Nanda Maharaja's elder brothers?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Upananda and Abhinanda as the elder brothers of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are described as the younger brothers of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text lists Sannanda and Nandana as the younger brothers of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific familial relationship that Upananda, Abhinanda, Sannanda, and Nandana hold in relation to Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by clarifying that these four individuals are the parental uncles of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many brothers of Nanda Maharaja are specifically identified by name as Krsna's parental uncles?
questions.explanation: The text lists four names: Upananda, Abhinanda, Sannanda, and Nandana.

options: They are all elder to Nanda Maharaja
They are all younger to Nanda Maharaja
Two are elder and two are younger
They are all cousins of Nanda Maharaja
questions.correct_answer: Two are elder and two are younger
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Upananda and Abhinanda are elder brothers, while Sannanda and Nandana are younger brothers.

1.33.verse_text: Upananda's complexion is pink. He has a long beard and wears green garments. Tungi-devi is his dear wife. Both her complexion and garments are the colour of a cataka bird.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Upananda, the elder brother of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Upananda's complexion is pink.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by Upananda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies green as the color of the garments worn by Upananda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the devoted wife of Upananda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Tungi-devi as the dear wife of Upananda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color is used to describe both the complexion and the garments of Tungi-devi?
options: The color of a cataka bird
The color of a pink lotus
The color of a blue cloud
The color of a bright conchshell
questions.correct_answer: The color of a cataka bird
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Tungi-devi's complexion and garments are the color of a cataka bird.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which physical feature is noted about Upananda's appearance besides his complexion and clothing?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that Upananda has a long beard.

1.34.verse_text: Abhinanda wears dark garments and his long beautiful beard is like a great conchshell. Pivari-devi is his wife. She wears blue garments and her complexion is pink.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the unique physical appearance of the long beard worn by Abhinanda?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Abhinanda's long beautiful beard is like a great conchshell.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by Abhinanda?

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the wife of Abhinanda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Pivari-devi as the wife of Abhinanda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of Pivari-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Pivari-devi's complexion is pink.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color of garments does Pivari-devi wear?
questions.explanation: The text states that Pivari-devi wears blue garments.

1.35.verse_text: Sannanda is also known as Sunanda. His complexion is white and he wears dark-colored clothing. He has two or three white hairs and he is very dear to Lord Kesava.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what other name is Sannanda known?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Sannanda is also known as Sunanda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the bodily complexion of Sannanda?
questions.explanation: Sannanda is described as having a white complexion.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by Sannanda?

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many white hairs are noted as belonging to Sannanda?

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to whom is Sannanda very dear?
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by stating that Sannanda is very dear to Lord Kesava.

1.36.verse_text: Kuvalaya-devi is Sannanda's wife. Her complexion is the colour of a blue lotus flower and she wears red garments. Nandana wears garments the color of a candata flower and his complexion is like the color of a peacock.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Kuvalaya-devi?
options: Color of a blue lotus flower
questions.correct_answer: Color of a blue lotus flower
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kuvalaya-devi's complexion is the color of a blue lotus flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which devotee is identified as the wife of Sannanda?
questions.explanation: The verse clearly identifies Kuvalaya-devi as the wife of Sannanda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by Kuvalaya-devi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Kuvalaya-devi as wearing red garments.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Nandana?
options: Like the color of a peacock
questions.correct_answer: Like the color of a peacock
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Nandana's complexion is like the color of a peacock.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by Nandana?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Nandana wears garments the color of a candata flower.

1.37.verse_text: Nandana lives in the same house with his father, Parjanya Maharaja. He is full of love for his yound nephew Krsna. Atulya-devi is Nandana's wife. Her complexion is like lightning and her garments resemble a dark cloud.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which elder relative does Nandana live with in the same house?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Nandana resides in the same house with his father, Parjanya Maharaja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the nature of Nandana's relationship with his young nephew, Krsna?
options: He is full of love for Him
He is fearful of His mystic powers
He is indifferent to His childhood play
He is strictly responsible for His education
questions.correct_answer: He is full of love for Him
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Nandana is full of love for his young nephew, Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Nandana's wife, Atulya-devi?
questions.explanation: It is stated that Atulya-devi's complexion resembles the brilliance of lightning.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by Atulya-devi?
questions.explanation: The text describes that the garments worn by Atulya-devi resemble the color of a dark cloud.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the wife of Nandana?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Atulya-devi as the wife of Nandana.

1.38.verse_text: Krsna's father has two sisters, Sananda-devi and Nandini-devi. They both dress in garments of many different colors. They have beautiful teeth and their complexions are the color of white foam. Sananda-devi's husband is Mahanila and Nandini's husband is Sunila.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of the two sisters of Krsna's father?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies Krsna's father's two sisters as Sananda-devi and Nandini-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the complexion of Krsna's paternal aunts, Sananda-devi and Nandini-devi, described?
options: The color of a lotus flower
questions.explanation: The text states that Sananda-devi and Nandini-devi possess complexions described as the color of white foam.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what describes the attire worn by the two sisters of Krsna's father?
options: Garments the color of a dark cloud
questions.explanation: It is stated that Sananda-devi and Nandini-devi dress themselves in garments of many different colors.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the husband of Sananda-devi, the aunt of Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text clearly identifies Mahanila as the husband of Sananda-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the husband of Nandini-devi, Krsna's paternal aunt?
questions.explanation: The scripture states that Sunila is the husband of Nandini-devi.

1.39-40.verse_text: Kandava and Dandava are the two sons of Upananda. Their faces are as beautiful as lotus flowers and they become especially happy in the association of their friend Subala. Catu and Batuka are Nanda Maharaja's two ksatriya cousins born in Vasudeva Maharaja's dynasty. Catu's wife is Dadhisara and Batuka's wife is Havihsara.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the two sons of Upananda noted for having faces as beautiful as lotus flowers?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kandava and Dandava are the two sons of Upananda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in whose association do Kandava and Dandava find special happiness?
questions.explanation: The text notes that Kandava and Dandava become especially happy in the association of their friend Subala.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, from which dynasty do the ksatriya cousins of Nanda Maharaja, named Catu and Batuka, originate?
questions.explanation: The text describes Catu and Batuka as Nanda Maharaja's two ksatriya cousins born in Vasudeva Maharaja's dynasty.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the wife of Catu, one of Nanda Maharaja's cousins?
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that Catu's wife is Dadhisara.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the wife of Batuka?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Havihsara as the wife of Batuka.

1.41.verse_text: Krsna's energetic and enthusiastic maternal grandfather is named Sumukha. His long beard is like a great conchshell and his complexion is the color of a ripe jambu fruit.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Krsna's energetic and enthusiastic maternal grandfather?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krsna's energetic and enthusiastic maternal grandfather is named Sumukha.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to what object is the long beard of Krsna's maternal grandfather, Sumukha, compared?
questions.explanation: The text describes Sumukha's long beard as being like a great conchshell.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the complexion of Krsna's maternal grandfather, Sumukha?
options: The color of a ripe jambu fruit
The color of a golden champaka flower
The color of a dark monsoon cloud
The color of a fresh lotus petal
questions.correct_answer: The color of a ripe jambu fruit
questions.explanation: The verse states that Sumukha's complexion is the color of a ripe jambu fruit.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which personality trait is specifically attributed to Krsna's maternal grandfather, Sumukha?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sumukha as being energetic and enthusiastic.

1.42.verse_text: Queen Patala-devi is Krsna's maternal grandmother and she is very famous in Vrajabhumi. Her hair is the white color of yogurt, her complexion is the pink color of a patala flower and her garments are green.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Krsna's maternal grandmother who is highly renowned within Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies Queen Patala-devi as Krsna's maternal grandmother and notes her fame in Vrajabhumi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific color of the hair of Krsna's grandmother, Patala-devi?
options: The grey color of a storm cloud

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which flower describes the pinkish complexion of Queen Patala-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse describes Patala-devi's complexion as having the pink color of a patala flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by Krsna's maternal grandmother, Patala-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse directly specifies that Patala-devi's garments are green.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what royal title is attributed to Krsna's maternal grandmother, Patala-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse opens by referring to her as Queen Patala-devi.

1.43.verse_text: Patala-devi's dear friend is named Mukhara-gopi. Out of intense affection for her friend, Mukhara used to feed the infant Yasoda with her own breast milk.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the dear friend of Krsna's maternal grandmother, Patala-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Patala-devi's dear friend is named Mukhara-gopi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which intimate service did Mukhara-gopi perform for baby Yasoda out of deep affection for her friend?
options: She fed her with her own breast milk
questions.correct_answer: She fed her with her own breast milk
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Mukhara used to feed the infant Yasoda with her own breast milk due to her intense affection for her friend Patala-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the primary motivation behind Mukhara-gopi's act of nursing the infant Yasoda?
options: Intense affection for her friend Patala-devi
questions.correct_answer: Intense affection for her friend Patala-devi
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the service of feeding Yasoda was performed out of intense affection for her friend, Patala-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose infant form was nourished by the breast milk of Mukhara-gopi?
questions.explanation: The text clearly states that Mukhara-gopi fed the infant Yasoda.

1.44-45.verse_text: Carumukha is Sumukha's younger brother. His complexion is the color of black eye-cosmetics and his wife Balaka-gopi was raised by her stepparents. Gola is the brother of Patala-devi, Krsna's maternal grandmother. His garments are the color of smoke. His brother-in-law, Sumukha, used to make fun of him and laugh and because of this Gola was very angry with him. Because he had worshiped Durvasa Muni in his previous birth, Gola was allowed to take birth in Vrajabhumi in the family of Ujjvala.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of Carumukha?
options: The color of a fresh monsoon cloud
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Carumukha's complexion as being the color of black eye-cosmetics.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the older brother of Carumukha?
questions.explanation: Carumukha is described in the text as the younger brother of Sumukha.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by Gola?
options: The color of deep forest green
questions.explanation: The text states that Gola's garments are the color of smoke.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the familial relationship between Gola and Patala-devi?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Gola as the brother of Patala-devi, who is Krsna's maternal grandmother.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which sage did Gola worship in his previous birth, enabling his appearance in the family of Ujjvala?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Gola was able to take birth in Vrajabhumi in the family of Ujjvala as a result of worshiping Durvasa Muni in his previous birth.

1.46.verse_text: Jatila-devi is Gola's wife. She has a large belly and her complexion is the color of a cow. Her sons Yasodhara, Yasodeva and Sudeva head the group of Krsna's maternal uncles.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the wife of the cowherd Gola?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Jatila-devi as the wife of Gola.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the distinctive physical feature of Jatila-devi?
options: A golden mark on her forehead
questions.explanation: The text describes Jatila-devi as having a large belly.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of Jatila-devi's complexion?
options: The color of a dark atasi flower
questions.explanation: The verse specifically states that the complexion of Jatila-devi is the color of a cow.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following are the sons of Jatila-devi?
questions.explanation: The text lists Yasodhara, Yasodeva and Sudeva as the sons of Jatila-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the group of Krsna's maternal uncles is headed by which individuals?
questions.explanation: The text states that Yasodhara, Yasodeva and Sudeva head the group of Krsna's maternal uncles.

1.47.verse_text: Krsna's maternal uncles have complexions like the color of a dark atasi flower and they dress in white garments. Their wives have complexions like karkati flowers and they dress in smoke-colored garments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the complexion of Krishna's maternal uncles?
options: The color of a dark atasi flower
The color of a yellow campaka flower
The color of a fresh lotus petal
The color of a golden champaka
questions.correct_answer: The color of a dark atasi flower
questions.explanation: The text states that Krishna's maternal uncles have complexions like the color of a dark atasi flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by the maternal uncles of Lord Krishna?
questions.explanation: The scripture specifies that Krishna's maternal uncles dress in white garments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of the wives of Krishna's maternal uncles?
options: Like the color of a dark atasi flower
Like the color of a karkati flower
Like the color of a bright golden lotus
Like the color of a pale jasmine bud
questions.correct_answer: Like the color of a karkati flower
questions.explanation: The text describes the wives of the maternal uncles as having complexions like karkati flowers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific color of the clothing worn by the wives of Krishna's maternal uncles?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the wives of the maternal uncles dress in smoke-colored garments.

1.48.verse_text: Rema-devi, Roma-devi and Surema-devi are the daughters of Krsna's parental uncle Pavana. Yasodevi and Yasasvini are the sisters of Krsna's mother Yasoda. Yasasvini's husband is named Malla.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the three daughters of Krsna's parental uncle, Pavana?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly identifies Rema-devi, Roma-devi, and Surema-devi as the daughters of Krsna's parental uncle, Pavana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are described as the sisters of Krsna's mother, Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Yasodevi and Yasasvini specifically as the sisters of Mother Yasoda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the husband of Krsna's maternal aunt, Yasasvini?
questions.explanation: The verse states clearly that Yasasvini's husband is named Malla.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the father of Rema-devi, Roma-devi, and Surema-devi?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Pavana, a parental uncle of Krsna, as the father of Rema-devi, Roma-devi, and Surema-devi.

1.49.verse_text: Yasodevi is elder. Her complexion is dark and she is also known by the name Dadhisara. Yasasvini is younger. Her complexion is fair and she is also known by the name Havihsara. Both dress in vermillion-colored garments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the two sisters of Mother Yasoda is recognized as the elder sibling?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Yasodevi is the elder sister of Mother Yasoda.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the alternative name by which Yasodevi, the elder sister of Mother Yasoda, is known?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the elder sister, Yasodevi, as being also known by the name Dadhisara.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Mother Yasoda's younger sister, Yasasvini?
questions.explanation: The scripture describes Yasasvini as having a fair complexion.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the secondary name assigned to Yasasvini, the younger sister of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text records that the younger sister, Yasasvini, is known by the name Havihsara.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific color of the garments worn by the sisters Yasodevi and Yasasvini?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that both sisters, Yasodevi and Yasasvini, dress in vermillion-colored garments.

1.50.verse_text: Yasoda's sisters married the two ksatriyas Catu and Batuka (see text 40). Yasoda's uncle Carumukha has one son who is named Sucaru.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to which two ksatriyas did the sisters of Mother Yasoda get married?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Yasoda's sisters were married to the two ksatriyas named Catu and Batuka.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the son born to Yasoda's uncle, Carumukha?
questions.explanation: The text identifies that Yasoda's uncle, Carumukha, has one son named Sucaru.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the familial relationship between Carumukha and Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Carumukha as the uncle of Mother Yasoda.

1.51.verse_text: Sucaru's wife is Tulavati-devi, the daughter of Sumukha's brother-in-law Gola. Tundu, Kutera, Purata and others are the comtemporary associates of Krsna's parental grandfather.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the wife of Sucaru?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Sucaru's wife is Tulavati-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, Tulavati-devi is mentioned as the daughter of which individual?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Tulavati-devi as the daughter of Sumukha's brother-in-law, Gola.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, Gola is described as being the brother-in-law of whom?
questions.explanation: The text states that Tulavati-devi is the daughter of Sumukha's brother-in-law, Gola.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are mentioned among the contemporary associates of Krsna's parental grandfather?
questions.explanation: The text lists Tundu, Kutera, Purata, and others as the contemporary associates of Krsna's parental grandfather.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the individuals Tundu, Kutera, and Purata hold what specific relationship or status to Krsna?
options: Contemporary associates of His parental grandfather
Contemporary associates of His maternal grandfather
questions.correct_answer: Contemporary associates of His parental grandfather
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Tundu, Kutera, and Purata as the contemporary associates of Krsna's parental grandfather.

1.52.verse_text: Kila, Antakela, Tilata, Krpita, Purata, Gonda, Kallota, Karanda, Tarisana, Varisana, Viraroha and Vararoha are among the contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandfather.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandfather?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists Antakela as one of the contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandfather.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group of individuals is specifically identified as being the contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandfather?
questions.explanation: Kila, Antakela, and Tilata are all part of the list of contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandfather provided in the text.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the associate named Vararoha categorized in relation to the residents of Vraja?
options: As a contemporary associate of Krsna's maternal grandfather
As a contemporary associate of Krsna's parental grandmother
As a member of Krsna's parental grandfather's circle
As a contemporary of Krsna's father
questions.correct_answer: As a contemporary associate of Krsna's maternal grandfather
questions.explanation: Vararoha is listed as one of the contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandfather.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is identified as a contemporary associate of Krsna's maternal grandfather, alongside Viraroha?
questions.explanation: Both Varisana and Viraroha are listed among the contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandfather.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which pair of names appears as associates of Krsna's maternal grandfather?
questions.explanation: Gonda and Kallota are specifically named in the sequence of associates for Krsna's maternal grandfather.

1.53.verse_text: The elderly ladies Silabheri, Sikhambara, Bharuni, Bhangur, Bhangi, Bharasakha and Sikha are among the contemporary associates of Krsna's parental grandmother.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which elderly lady is listed among the contemporary associates of Krsna's parental grandmother?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Silabheri as one of the elderly ladies who are contemporary associates of Krsna's parental grandmother.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is identified as one of the contemporary associates of Krsna's parental grandmother?
questions.explanation: The text lists Bharasakha among the specific elderly ladies associated with Krsna's parental grandmother.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who among the following is described as an elderly lady and contemporary associate of Krsna's parental grandmother?
questions.explanation: Bhangur is explicitly mentioned in the verse as an elderly associate of the parental grandmother.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which lady is mentioned alongside Sikhambara and Bharuni as a contemporary associate of Krsna's parental grandmother?
questions.explanation: The verse includes Sikha as one of the elderly ladies serving as a contemporary associate of Krsna's parental grandmother.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these is one of the elderly ladies belonging to the circle of Krsna's parental grandmother's contemporary associates?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Bhangi as one of the elderly ladies associated with the parental grandmother of Krsna.

1.54-55.verse_text: Bharunda, Jatila, Bhela, Karala, Karabalika, Gharghara, Mukhara, Ghora, Ghanta, Ghoni, Sughantika, Dhvankarunti, Handi, Tundi, Dingima, Manjuranika, Cakkini, Condika, Cundi, Dindima, Pundavanika, Damani, Damari, Dambi and Danka are among the contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandmother.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed among the contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandmother?
questions.explanation: Bharunda is explicitly identified as one of the associates of Krsna's maternal grandmother in the text.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names is mentioned as one of the ladies who associates with Krsna's maternal grandmother?
questions.explanation: Jatila is listed as an associate of Krsna's maternal grandmother alongside others like Bhela and Karala.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which lady among the following is categorized as a contemporary associate of Krsna's maternal grandmother?
questions.explanation: The text lists Mukhara specifically as a contemporary associate of Krsna's maternal grandmother.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these is one of the associates serving in the group of Krsna's maternal grandmother?
questions.explanation: Ghanta is mentioned in the list of associates of Krsna's maternal grandmother, distinct from the parental grandmother's associates.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following figures is named as a contemporary associate of Krsna's maternal grandmother?
questions.explanation: Dindima is included in the specific list of ladies who are contemporary associates of Krsna's maternal grandmother.

1.56-58.verse_text: Mangala, Pingala, Pinga, Mathura, Pitha, Pattisa, Sankara, Sangara, Bhrngaq, Ghrni, Ghatika, Saragha, Pathira, Dandi, Kedara, Saurabheya, Kala, Ankura, Dhurina, Dhurva, Cakranga, Maskara, Utpala, Kambala, Supaksa, Saudha, Harita, Harikesa, Hara and Upananda are among the contemporary associates of Krsna's father, Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these personalities is identified as a contemporary associate of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists Upananda among the contemporary associates of Krsna's father, Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following individuals is mentioned as a companion of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Kedara is named as one of the contemporary associates of Nanda Maharaja in the provided verses.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who among the following is included in the list of associates of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Harikesa is listed specifically as one of the contemporary associates of Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which associate is listed alongside Mangala and Pingala as a friend of Nanda Maharaja?
questions.explanation: Sankara appears in the text as one of the specific contemporaries and associates associated with Nanda Maharaja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names belongs to the group of contemporary associates of Krsna's father?
questions.explanation: Supaksa is clearly identified as one of the associates of Nanda Maharaja in the catalog provided by the text.

1.59.verse_text: Having taken a vow of friendship in their youth, Parjanya and Sumukha always remain the best of friends. Nanda Maharaja and many other of the cowherds are descended from their families.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who took a vow of friendship with Parjanya during their youth?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Parjanya and Sumukha took a vow of friendship in their youth and remain the best of friends.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the nature of the relationship between Parjanya and Sumukha?
options: They are the best of friends
They are rivals for the leadership of the cowherds
questions.correct_answer: They are the best of friends
questions.explanation: The text describes Parjanya and Sumukha as having taken a vow of friendship in their youth and maintaining that bond.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, when did Parjanya and Sumukha take their vow of friendship?
options: During the time of Krsna's birth
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that they took this vow of friendship while they were still in their youth.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, from whose ancestral families are Nanda Maharaja and the other cowherds descended?
questions.explanation: The text states that Nanda Maharaja and many other cowherds are descendants of the families of Parjanya and Sumukha.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which prominent cowherd is specifically mentioned as being descended from the families of Parjanya and Sumukha?
questions.explanation: The verse clearly names Nanda Maharaja as one of the cowherds who descends from the lineage of Parjanya and Sumukha.

1.60-62.verse_text: Vatsala, Kusala, Tali, Medura, Masrna, Krpa, Sankini, Bimbini, Mitra, Subhaga, Bhogini, Prabha, Sarika Hingula, Niti, Kapila, Dhamanidhara, Paksati, Pataka, Pundi, Sutunda, Tusti, Anjana, Tarangaksi, Taralika, Subhada, Malika, Angada, Visala, Sallaki, Vena and Vartika are among the contemporary associates of Krsna's mother, Yasoda-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the contemporary associates of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: Vatsala is explicitly listed in the text as one of the contemporary associates of Krsna's mother, Yasoda-devi.
