questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which individual is named as one of the associates who accompanies Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: Sutunda is identified in the list of contemporary associates belonging to Mother Yasoda-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names is explicitly mentioned among the group of associates of Krsna's mother?
questions.explanation: Taralika is included in the provided scriptural list of the contemporary associates of Mother Yasoda.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is identified as an associate of Mother Yasoda?
questions.explanation: Paksati is listed by name as a contemporary associate of Mother Yasoda-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which name is recognized as belonging to the circle of Mother Yasoda's contemporary associates?
questions.explanation: Vartika is clearly mentioned in the sequence of names identifying the contemporary associates of Mother Yasoda-devi.

1.63.verse_text: Ambika and Kilimba are the two nurses who fed Krsna with their breast milk. Of the two, Ambika, the dear friend of Vraja's queen, is the most important.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two nurses had the special privilege of feeding Krishna with their breast milk?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Ambika and Kilimba are the two nurses who fed Krishna with their breast milk.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is described as the dear friend of Vraja's queen, Queen Yasoda?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Ambika as the dear friend of Vraja's queen.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, among the two nurses Ambika and Kilimba, who is considered the most important?
options: Neither is mentioned as most important
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that of the two nurses, Ambika is the most important.

1.64-65.verse_text: The Brahmanas: Two groups of brahmanas reside in Gokula : those maintained by Nanda Maharaja and those who are priests engaged in the performance of Vedic sacrifices. Among the priests, Vedagarbha, Mahayajva and Bhaguri are prominent. Their respective wives are Samadheni, Mahakavya and Vedika.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the two groups of brahmanas residing in Gokula classified?
options: Those maintained by Nanda Maharaja and those who are priests engaged in Vedic sacrifices
Those born in Mathura and those who migrated from the northern forests
Those who serve the forest deities and those who serve the Yamuna banks
Those who chant the Gayatri and those who specialize in Tantric rituals
questions.correct_answer: Those maintained by Nanda Maharaja and those who are priests engaged in Vedic sacrifices
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that two groups of brahmanas reside in Gokula: those maintained by Nanda Maharaja and those serving as priests for Vedic sacrifices.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the three prominent brahmanas serving as priests in Gokula?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Vedagarbha, Mahayajva, and Bhaguri as the prominent priests engaged in Vedic sacrifices.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the wife of the brahmana priest known as Vedagarbha?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the wives of the prominent priests Vedagarbha, Mahayajva, and Bhaguri are Samadheni, Mahakavya, and Vedika respectively.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which brahmana lady is mentioned as the wife of the priest named Mahayajva?
questions.explanation: The text correlates the wives Samadheni, Mahakavya, and Vedika to the priests Vedagarbha, Mahayajva, and Bhaguri in order.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the wife of the prominent brahmana priest, Bhaguri?
questions.explanation: The text lists Vedika as the wife associated with the priest Bhaguri.

1.66.verse_text: Sulabha, Gautami, Gargi, Candila, Kubjika, Vamani, Svaha, Sulata, Sandili, Svadha and Bhargavi are the most important of the brahmana ladies respectfully worshiped by the residents of Vraja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these saintly brahmana ladies is specifically listed among the most important women respectfully worshiped by the residents of Vraja?
questions.explanation: Sulabha is explicitly mentioned in the list of the most important brahmana ladies worshiped by the residents of Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which lady named alongside Gautami and Gargi is honored as a prominent brahmana woman in Vraja?
questions.explanation: Candila is listed as one of the important brahmana ladies within the group including Gautami and Gargi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who among the following is included in the list of eleven primary brahmana ladies worshiped by the inhabitants of Vrajabhumi?
questions.explanation: Kubjika is clearly identified as one of the eleven most important brahmana ladies worshiped by the residents of Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides Bhargavi and Sandili, which of these figures is also recognized as an esteemed brahmana lady of Vraja?
questions.explanation: Vamani is explicitly mentioned as one of the group of eleven significant brahmana ladies respected by the Vrajavasis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names belongs to the group of brahmana ladies that includes Svadha and Svaha?
questions.explanation: Sulata is included in the list of the eleven primary brahmana ladies along with Svadha and Svaha.

1.67-69.verse_text: Full of transcendental opulences, Paurnamasi-devi is the incarnation of the Lord's Yogamaya potency. She makes the arrangements of Lord Krsna's pastimes to be properly performed. She is slightly tall in stature and has a fair complexion. Her hair is the color of kasa flowers and she wears red garments. She is greatly respected by Nanda Maharaja and all the other residents of Vrajabhumi. She was the dear student of Devarsi Narada and on his advice she left her favorite son, Sandipani Muni, in Avantipura and came to Gokula, impelled by great love for her worshipable Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, of which divine potency is Paurnamasi-devi considered the incarnation?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Paurnamasi-devi as the incarnation of the Lord's Yogamaya potency.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color are the garments worn by the revered Paurnamasi-devi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Paurnamasi-devi as wearing red garments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the hair of the transcendental Paurnamasi-devi?
questions.explanation: The text states that her hair is the color of kasa flowers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is mentioned as the son whom Paurnamasi-devi left behind in Avantipura?
questions.explanation: The text notes that on the advice of Narada, she left her favorite son, Sandipani Muni, in Avantipura.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who was the teacher that advised Paurnamasi-devi to come to Gokula?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Devarsi Narada as the one whose advice led her to come to Gokula.

1.70.verse_text: Krsna's associates may be divided into two groups : gopis and gopas. The gopis may be divided into three groups : 1. Gopi friends of the same age as Krsna, 2. maidservants and 3. gopi messengers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, into which two primary groups are the associates of Lord Krsna divided?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krsna's associates are divided into these two groups: gopis and gopas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, into how many distinct categories are the gopis divided?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the gopis are divided into three groups: friends of the same age, maidservants, and messengers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the three groups of gopis?
options: Gopi friends of the same age as Krsna
The gopis who live in Mathura
questions.correct_answer: Gopi friends of the same age as Krsna
questions.explanation: The text lists 'Gopi friends of the same age as Krsna' as the first of the three categories of gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which role is specifically included in the three-fold classification of the gopis?
options: Gopi cowherd girls from other villages
questions.explanation: The verse identifies 'maidservants' as the second group among the three divisions of gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the third category defined under the grouping of gopis?
questions.explanation: The verse concludes the list of the three gopi groups by mentioning 'gopi messengers'.

1.71-72.verse_text: Learned scholars have also divided Lord Krsna's associates into the following nine categories : 1. yutha, 2. kula, 3. mandala, 4. varga, 5. gana, 6. samavaya, 7. sancaya, 8. samaja, and 9. and samanvaya.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many distinct categories are mentioned by learned scholars for classifying Lord Krsna's associates?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that learned scholars have divided the associates of Lord Krsna into nine categories.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the nine categories for classifying Lord Krsna's associates?
questions.explanation: Yutha is the first of the nine categories listed in the classification of the Lord's associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following terms is included among the nine categories identified for Lord Krsna's associates?
questions.explanation: Kula is identified as the second of the nine specific categories for classifying the Lord's associates.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is correctly identified as one of the categories of Krsna's associates?
questions.explanation: Mandala is explicitly named as the third category in the list of nine divisions of the Lord's associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these is counted among the nine categories of the Lord's associates?
questions.explanation: Samaja is the eighth category among the nine listed for classifying Lord Krsna's associates.

1.73-75.verse_text: Krsna's Gopi Friends : Of the three kinds of gopi associates of the Lord mentioned in text 70, the first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, are the most exalted, the second group, the maidservants are the next most exalted, and the gopi messengers come after them. These three groups correspond to the groups mentioned as samaja, mandala and gana respectively (in texts 71 - 72). The first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, may be further divided into varistha (the most exalted ) and vara (exalted).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, among the three classes of gopi associates of Lord Krishna, which group is considered the most exalted?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the first group, Krsna's contemporary gopi friends, are the most exalted of the three kinds of gopi associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which classification corresponds to the gopi messengers?
questions.explanation: The text states that the three groups of gopi associates correspond to the samaja, mandala, and gana categories respectively, with the messengers being the third group (gana).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the gopi maidservants ranked in terms of exaltation compared to the other two groups?
questions.explanation: The text describes the contemporary friends as the most exalted, the maidservants as the next most exalted, and the messengers as coming after them.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the two further divisions of the contemporary gopi friends?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that the first group, the contemporary gopi friends, may be further divided into varistha (most exalted) and vara (exalted).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the gopi maidservants correspond to which category mentioned earlier in the text?
questions.explanation: The text correlates the three gopi groups (contemporary friends, maidservants, messengers) with the categories samaja, mandala, and gana in that specific order.

1.76.verse_text: Varistha (The Most Exalted Gopis): The varistha gopis are more famous than all the others. They are eternally the intimate friends of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna. No one can equal or exceed the love they bear for the divine couple.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the varistha gopis described in relation to the divine couple, Sri Sri Radha-Krsna?
options: They are eternally the intimate friends
questions.correct_answer: They are eternally the intimate friends
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the varistha gopis as being eternally the intimate friends of Sri Sri Radha-Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what unique status do the varistha gopis hold regarding their fame compared to other associates?
options: They are more famous than all the others
They are equal in fame to the messengers
They seek no fame in the assembly
They are less famous than the maidservants
questions.correct_answer: They are more famous than all the others
questions.explanation: The text states that the varistha gopis are more famous than all the other gopi associates.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the love of the varistha gopis for the divine couple compare to others?
options: No one can equal or exceed it
It is surpassed by the maidservants
It is equal to the gopi messengers
It is considered standard among devotees
questions.correct_answer: No one can equal or exceed it
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the love the varistha gopis bear for the divine couple is unparalleled, as no one can equal or exceed it.

1.77.verse_text: Of all Krsna's friends, the varistha gopis are the most worshipable. They are decorated with incomparable transcendental qualities, bodily beauty and all other charming opulences.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the varistha gopis described in relation to all of Krishna's friends?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that of all Krsna's friends, the varistha gopis are the most worshipable.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the varistha gopis said to be decorated with?
options: Incomparable transcendental qualities and bodily beauty
questions.correct_answer: Incomparable transcendental qualities and bodily beauty
questions.explanation: The text states that the varistha gopis are decorated with incomparable transcendental qualities, bodily beauty, and all other charming opulences.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides their beauty and qualities, what else distinguishes the varistha gopis?
questions.explanation: The verse mentions that in addition to their qualities and beauty, they are decorated with all other charming opulences.

1.78.verse_text: Krsna's Gopi Friends: Lalita, Visakha, Citra, Campakamallika, Tungavidya, Indulekha, Rangadevi and Sudevi are the most intimate of Lord Krsna's gopi friends.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group of associates is identified as the most intimate of Lord Krsna's gopi friends?
options: Lalita, Visakha, Citra, Campakamallika, Tungavidya, Indulekha, Rangadevi and Sudevi
Kusumalata, Ranganatya, Tulasi, Candravali, Madhavi, Lalita, Visakha and Citra
Sahadevi, Candrika, Madhumati, Padmavati, Lalita, Visakha, Tungavidya and Sudevi
Indulekha, Rangadevi, Sudevi, Tungavidya, Citra, Campakamallika, Visakha and Candravali
questions.correct_answer: Lalita, Visakha, Citra, Campakamallika, Tungavidya, Indulekha, Rangadevi and Sudevi
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists these eight gopis as the most intimate friends of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many gopis are specifically enumerated as the most intimate friends of Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse names eight specific gopis: Lalita, Visakha, Citra, Campakamallika, Tungavidya, Indulekha, Rangadevi and Sudevi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed among the most intimate gopi friends of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: Campakamallika is one of the eight intimate gopi friends identified in the verse.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, is Rangadevi considered one of the most intimate gopi friends of Krsna?
options: No, she is a kinswoman of the gopas
No, she is only a friend of Radharani
questions.explanation: Rangadevi is directly named in the list of eight most intimate gopi friends of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these is NOT part of the group of eight most intimate gopi friends of Krsna?
questions.explanation: While Srimati Radharani is the supreme gopi, she is not included in the specific list of the eight gopi friends mentioned in this verse.

1.79.verse_text: Lalita: Of these eight gopis, the first one, Lalita-devi, is the best. She is a dear friend of the divine couple and she is 27 years old, the eldest of Krsna's gopi friends.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how old is Lalita-devi?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Lalita-devi as being 27 years old.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the eight primary gopi friends is described as the eldest?
questions.explanation: The text states that among the eight gopis, Lalita-devi is the eldest.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the status of Lalita-devi among the eight gopi friends characterized?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Lalita-devi as the best among the eight gopis mentioned.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to whom is Lalita-devi considered a dear friend?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Lalita-devi as a dear friend of the divine couple.

1.80.verse_text: Lalita is famous as Srimati Radharani's constant companion and follower. Lalita is contrary and hot-tempered by nature. Her complexion is like the yellow pigment gorocana and her garments are like peacock feathers.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific nature of the temperament displayed by Lalita-devi?
options: She is perpetually compassionate and soft-hearted
She is grave and difficult to understand
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Lalita-devi as being contrary and hot-tempered by nature.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to which precious substance is the complexion of Lalita-devi compared?
options: The rays of the rising sun
questions.explanation: The text states that the complexion of Lalita-devi is similar to the color of yellow gorocana pigment.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what visual pattern or color scheme do the garments of Lalita-devi resemble?
questions.explanation: The text describes Lalita-devi's garments as appearing like the vibrant colors of peacock feathers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary role of Lalita-devi in relation to Srimati Radharani?
options: She is Her constant companion and follower
She is Her advisor in culinary matters
She is Her messenger to the forest gopis
She is Her childhood playmate from Varshana
questions.correct_answer: She is Her constant companion and follower
questions.explanation: The text identifies Lalita-devi specifically as the constant companion and follower of Srimati Radharani.

1.81.verse_text: Her mother is Saradi-devi and her father is Visoka. Her husband is named Bhairava. He is a close friend of Govardhana-gopa.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the mother of the gopi Lalita?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Lalita's mother is Saradi-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Lalita's father?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Visoka is the father of Lalita.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is mentioned as the husband of Lalita?
questions.explanation: Lalita's husband is explicitly named as Bhairava in the text.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, Bhairava, the husband of Lalita, is noted for being a close friend of which individual?
questions.explanation: The text states that Bhairava is a close friend of Govardhana-gopa.

1.82-83.verse_text: Visakha: Visakha is the second most important of these eight varistha-gopis. Her attributes, activities and resolve are all muck like those of her friend Lalita. Visakha was born at the exact same moment as her dear friend Srimati Radharani appeared in this world. Visakha's garments are decorated with stars and her complexion is like lightning. Her father is Pavana, the son of the sister of Mukhara-gopi and her mother is Daksina-devi, the daughter of the sister of Jatila. Her husband is Vahika-gopa.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the significance of the timing of Visakha-gopi's birth?
options: She was born at the exact same moment as Srimati Radharani
She was born at the moment the sun rose over Govardhana Hill
She was born one day after Srimati Radharani appeared
She was born at the very moment of Krishna's birth in Mathura
questions.correct_answer: She was born at the exact same moment as Srimati Radharani
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Visakha was born at the exact same moment as her dear friend Srimati Radharani appeared in this world.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is described as the pattern decorating the garments of Visakha?
questions.explanation: The text describes that Visakha's garments are decorated with stars.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Visakha-gopi?
options: Like the color of a blue-jay's feather
Like the color of a blossoming campaka flower
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Visakha's complexion is like lightning.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the husband of Visakha?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Vahika-gopa as the husband of Visakha.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the mother of Visakha?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Daksina-devi, the daughter of the sister of Jatila, as Visakha's mother.

1.84.verse_text: Campakalata: Campakalata is the third of the varistha-gopis. Her complexion is the color of a blossoming yellow campaka flower and her garments are the color of a blue-jay's feature. She is one day younger than Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of the varistha-gopi Campakalata?
options: The color of a blossoming yellow campaka flower
The color of a red lotus
The color of a dark monsoon cloud
questions.correct_answer: The color of a blossoming yellow campaka flower
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Campakalata's complexion as being the color of a blossoming yellow campaka flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which color are the garments worn by the gopi Campakalata?
options: The color of a blue-jay's feather
The color of a blossoming yellow campaka flower
The color of a dark forest vine
The color of the rising sun
questions.correct_answer: The color of a blue-jay's feather
questions.explanation: The text identifies the color of Campakalata's garments as matching the color of a blue-jay's feather.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the age relationship between Campakalata and Srimati Radharani?
options: She is one day younger than Srimati Radharani
She is the same age as Srimati Radharani
She is one day older than Srimati Radharani
She is two days younger than Srimati Radharani
questions.correct_answer: She is one day younger than Srimati Radharani
questions.explanation: The text states that Campakalata is one day younger than Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the hierarchical position of Campakalata among the varistha-gopis?
options: She is the third of the varistha-gopis
She is the first of the varistha-gopis
She is the second of the varistha-gopis
She is the fourth of the varistha-gopis
questions.correct_answer: She is the third of the varistha-gopis
questions.explanation: The text introduces Campakalata as the third of the varistha-gopis.

1.85.verse_text: Her husband is Arama, her mother is Vatika-devi and her husband is Candaksa. Her qualities are much like those of Visakha.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the mother of the gopi described in this specific sequence?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Vatika-devi as the mother of the gopi discussed in this section.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of the two individuals mentioned as husbands of the gopi being described?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the husbands of this specific gopi as Arama and Candaksa.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose qualities are stated to be very similar to those of the gopi being described in this verse?
questions.explanation: The text states that the qualities of this gopi are much like those of Visakha.

1.86.verse_text: Citra: Citra is the fourth of the varistha gopis. Her fair complexion resembles the color of kunkuma and her garments are the color of crystal. She is 26 days younger than Srimati Radharani. When Lord Madhava is full of blis, she becomes satisfied.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the complexion of the varistha gopi named Citra described?
options: The color of a monsoon cloud
The color of a lotus petal
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Citra's fair complexion resembles the color of kunkuma.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by the gopi Citra?
options: The color of a peacock feather
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Citra's garments are the color of crystal.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where is Citra ranked among the varistha gopis?
questions.explanation: The verse introduces Citra specifically as the fourth of the varistha gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many days younger is Citra than Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Citra is exactly 26 days younger than Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what event causes the gopi Citra to become satisfied?
options: When Lord Madhava is full of bliss
questions.correct_answer: When Lord Madhava is full of bliss
questions.explanation: The verse states that when Lord Madhava is full of bliss, Citra becomes satisfied.

1.87.verse_text: Her father is Catura, the paternal uncle of Suryamitra. Her mother is Carcika-devi. Her husband is Pithara.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the father of the gopi Citra?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Catura as the father of Citra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is mentioned as the husband of the gopi Citra?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that Pithara is the husband of Citra.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the mother of the gopi Citra?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Carcika-devi as the mother of Citra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the father of Citra, Catura, is related to whom as a paternal uncle?
questions.explanation: The text states that Catura is the paternal uncle of Suryamitra.

1.88.verse_text: Tungavidya: Tungavidya is the fifth of the varistha gopis. Her complexion is the color of kunkuma and the fragrance of her body is like sandalwood mixed with camphor. She is fifteen days younger than Srimati Radharani

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific rank among the varistha gopis is held by the sakhi known as Tungavidya?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Tungavidya as the fifth of the varistha gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of Tungavidya?
questions.explanation: The verse describes Tungavidya's complexion as being the color of kunkuma.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the delightful, specific scent of Tungavidya's bodily fragrance?
questions.explanation: The text characterizes the fragrance of Tungavidya's body as being like sandalwood mixed with camphor.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the exact age difference between Tungavidya and Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: It is stated that Tungavidya is fifteen days younger than Srimati Radharani.

1.89.verse_text: Tungavidya is hot-tempered and expert at dissimulation. She wears white garments. Her parents are Puskara and Medha-devi and her husband is Balisa.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the temperament of the gopi Tungavidya?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Tungavidya as hot-tempered.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by the gopi Tungavidya?
questions.explanation: Tungavidya is specifically noted to wear white garments.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Tungavidya's father?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Puskara as the father of Tungavidya.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the husband of the gopi Tungavidya?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Balisa as the husband of Tungavidya.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Tungavidya's mother?
questions.explanation: The text records Medha-devi as the mother of Tungavidya.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, for what specific behavior is the gopi Tungavidya noted besides being hot-tempered?
questions.explanation: The text describes Tungavidya as both hot-tempered and expert at dissimulation.

1.90.verse_text: Indulekha: Indulekha is the sixth of the varistha gopis. She has a tan complexion and wears garments the color of a pomegranate flower. She is three days younger than Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which position among the varistha gopis is held by Indulekha?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Indulekha as the sixth of the varistha gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion described for Indulekha?
questions.explanation: The verse describes Indulekha as having a tan complexion.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by Indulekha?
options: The color of a pomegranate flower
The color of a lotus petal
The color of a peacock feather
The color of a morning cloud
questions.correct_answer: The color of a pomegranate flower
questions.explanation: The text states that Indulekha wears garments the color of a pomegranate flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the age of Indulekha compared to Srimati Radharani?
options: She is three days older than Srimati Radharani
She is the same age as Srimati Radharani
She is three days younger than Srimati Radharani
She is seven days younger than Srimati Radharani
questions.correct_answer: She is three days younger than Srimati Radharani
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that Indulekha is three days younger than Srimati Radharani.

1.91.verse_text: Her parents are Sagara and Vela-devi and her husband is Durbala. She is contrary and hot-tempered by nature.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the parents of the gopi Indulekha?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Sagara as her father and Vela-devi as her mother.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Indulekha's husband?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Indulekha's husband is Durbala.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which personality trait describes the nature of Indulekha?
questions.explanation: The text characterizes Indulekha as having a contrary and hot-tempered nature.

1.92.verse_text: Rangadevi: Rangadevi is the seventh of the varistha gopis. Her complexion is the color of a lotus filament and her garments are the color of a red rose. She is seven days younger than Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific rank assigned to Rangadevi among the varistha gopis?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Rangadevi as the seventh of the varistha gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what hue resembles the bodily complexion of the beloved gopi Rangadevi?
questions.explanation: The text describes Rangadevi's complexion as being the color of a lotus filament.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by the gopi Rangadevi?
options: The color of a red rose
The color of a peacock feather
The color of a lotus filament
The color of a rain cloud
questions.correct_answer: The color of a red rose
questions.explanation: The text describes Rangadevi's garments as having the color of a red rose.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the exact age difference between the gopi Rangadevi and Srimati Radharani?
options: They are exactly the same age
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Rangadevi is seven days younger than Srimati Radharani.

1.93.verse_text: Her personal qualities are much like those of Campakalata. Her parents are Karuna-devi and Rangasara.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose personal qualities are described as being much like those of Rangadevi?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Rangadevi's personal qualities are much like those of Campakalata.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the mother of the gopi Rangadevi?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Karuna-devi as the mother of Rangadevi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the father of the gopi Rangadevi?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Rangasara is the father of Rangadevi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the names of the parents of the gopi Rangadevi?
questions.explanation: The text clearly identifies the parents of Rangadevi as Karuna-devi and Rangasara.

1.94.verse_text: Sudevi: Sudevi is the eighth of the varistha gopis. She is sweet and charming by nature. She is the sister of Rangadevi. Her husband is Vakreksana, the younger brother of Bhairava.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which position does the gopi Sudevi hold among the varistha-gopis?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Sudevi as the eighth of the varistha-gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the sister of the gopi Sudevi?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Rangadevi as the sister of Sudevi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the husband of the gopi Sudevi?
questions.explanation: Sudevi's husband is explicitly named as Vakreksana.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the family relationship between Sudevi's husband, Vakreksana, and Bhairava?
options: Vakreksana is the father of Bhairava
Vakreksana is the elder brother of Bhairava
Vakreksana is the younger brother of Bhairava
Vakreksana is the cousin of Bhairava
questions.correct_answer: Vakreksana is the younger brother of Bhairava
questions.explanation: The text states that Vakreksana is the younger brother of Bhairava.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the nature of the gopi Sudevi described?
questions.explanation: Sudevi is described as being sweet and charming by nature.

1.95.verse_text: Her marriage with Vakreksana was arranged by his younger brother. Her form and other qualities are so similar to those of her sister Rangadevi that they are often mistaken for one another.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which relative of her husband arranged the marriage of Sudevi to Vakreksana?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Sudevi's marriage to Vakreksana was arranged by his younger brother.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why is Sudevi frequently mistaken for her sister, Rangadevi?
options: Because their forms and qualities are very similar
Because they always dress in matching garments
Because they both serve the same gopi group
questions.correct_answer: Because their forms and qualities are very similar
questions.explanation: The text explains that Sudevi's form and other qualities are so similar to those of Rangadevi that they are often confused with one another.

1.96-97.verse_text: The Vara-Gopis: Just as there are eight varistha (most exalted) gopis, the same way there are also eight vara (exalted) gopis. The vara-gopis are all teenage girls. Their names are Kalavati, Subhangada, Hiranyangi, Ratnalekha, Sikhavati, Kandarpa manjari, Phullakalika and Ananga manjari

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which classification of gopis are described as all being teenage girls, similar in category to the varistha-gopis?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly defines the vara-gopis as teenage girls and notes there are eight of them, just as there are eight varistha-gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many gopis are categorized within the group known as the vara-gopis?
questions.explanation: The text states that just as there are eight varistha gopis, there are also eight vara gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the eight vara-gopis?
questions.explanation: Kandarpa-manjari is specifically listed in the text as one of the eight vara-gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which name is included among the eight exalted vara-gopis?
questions.explanation: Hiranyangi is identified by the text as one of the eight vara-gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names belongs to the group of the eight vara-gopis?
questions.explanation: Sikhavati is explicitly named in the text as one of the eight vara-gopis.

1.98.verse_text: Kalavati's parents are Sindhumati-devi and Kalankura-gopa, the maternal uncle of Arkamitra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the mother of the gopi Kalavati?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kalavati's parents are Sindhumati-devi and Kalankura-gopa.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the father of the gopi Kalavati?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Kalankura-gopa as the father of Kalavati.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to whom is Kalankura-gopa related as a maternal uncle?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Kalankura-gopa is the maternal uncle of Arkamitra.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the familial status of Kalankura-gopa in relation to Arkamitra?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly defines Kalankura-gopa as the maternal uncle of Arkamitra.

1.99.verse_text: Her complexion is the color of yellow sandalwood and she wears garments the color of a parrot. Her husband is Kapota, the youngest brother of Vahika.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of the gopi described as having garments the color of a parrot?
options: The color of a fresh lotus petal
The color of a golden champaka flower
The color of a dark monsoon cloud
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that her complexion is the color of yellow sandalwood.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by the gopi whose husband is Kapota?
options: The color of a ripe pomegranate
The color of a dark sapphire
The color of a golden bumblebee
questions.explanation: The text describes that she wears garments that are the color of a parrot.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the husband of the gopi whose complexion is the color of yellow sandalwood?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies her husband as Kapota.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the relationship of the gopi's husband, Kapota, to Vahika?
questions.explanation: The text states that Kapota is the youngest brother of Vahika.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, Vahika is the elder brother of which individual mentioned in the text?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Kapota as the youngest brother of Vahika.

1.100.verse_text: Subhangada: Subhangada is the younger sister of Visakha. She is a very fair complexion and is married to Patatri, the younger brother of Pithara.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which prominent sakhi is identified as the elder sister of Subhangada?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Subhangada is the younger sister of Visakha.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the bodily complexion of the gopi Subhangada?
questions.explanation: The verse describes Subhangada as having a very fair complexion.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the husband of the gopi Subhangada?
questions.explanation: The text states that Subhangada is married to Patatri.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the husband of Subhangada, Patatri, is the younger brother of which individual?
questions.explanation: The text notes that Patatri is the younger brother of Pithara.

1.101.verse_text: Hiranyangi: Hiranyangi's complexion is the color of gold and she appears to be a temple or palace in which all beauty is conserved. She was born from the womb of Harini-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of the beautiful Hiranyangi?
options: The color of a dark monsoon cloud
The color of a lotus petal
The color of a campaka flower
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Hiranyangi's complexion is the color of gold.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which exalted mother gave birth to Hiranyangi?
questions.explanation: The text clearly identifies that Hiranyangi was born from the womb of Harini-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to what structure is the beauty of Hiranyangi compared?
questions.explanation: The text describes Hiranyangi as appearing to be a temple or palace in which all beauty is conserved.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what quality is described as being conserved within the person of Hiranyangi?
questions.explanation: The verse states that Hiranyangi appears as a temple or palace in which all beauty is conserved.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following best describes the appearance of Hiranyangi?
options: A servant of the divine forest
questions.explanation: She is described as a temple or palace where all beauty is conserved, signifying her role as a repository of beauty.

1.102.verse_text: Mahavasu-gopa is pious, famous and devoted to performing Vedic sacrifices. He is decorated with wonderful good qualities and he is the close friend of Arkamitra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which prominent cowherd is described as being both pious and famous while being deeply devoted to performing Vedic sacrifices?
questions.explanation: Mahavasu-gopa is explicitly identified as the pious, famous, and sacrifice-performing cowherd.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the close friend of the pious and sacrificial Mahavasu-gopa?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Mahavasu-gopa is the close friend of Arkamitra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what primary characteristic, besides his fame and piety, defines the activity of Mahavasu-gopa?
options: Playing the flute in the forest
questions.explanation: Mahavasu-gopa is described as being devoted to performing Vedic sacrifices.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the nature of Mahavasu-gopa's personality described in relation to his personal attributes?
options: He is decorated with wonderful good qualities
He is known for his immense physical strength
He is the master of all forest pastimes
He is always found in the company of the Gopis
questions.correct_answer: He is decorated with wonderful good qualities
questions.explanation: The text describes Mahavasu-gopa as being decorated with wonderful good qualities.

1.103.verse_text: Mahavasu-gopa desired to beget a powerful and heroic son and a beautiful daughter. To attain this end the self-controlled Mahavasu engaged Bhaguri Muni in performing a Vedic sacrifice.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, for what specific reason did Mahavasu-gopa engage Bhaguri Muni in performing a Vedic sacrifice?
options: To obtain wealth and prosperity for the Vraja village
To beget a powerful, heroic son and a beautiful daughter
To ensure the protection of the cows from demons
To secure long life and health for his family
questions.correct_answer: To beget a powerful, heroic son and a beautiful daughter
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Mahavasu-gopa desired a powerful and heroic son and a beautiful daughter, leading him to engage Bhaguri Muni for the sacrifice.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who was the sage engaged by Mahavasu-gopa to perform the Vedic sacrifice intended for his progeny?
questions.explanation: Mahavasu-gopa specifically engaged Bhaguri Muni to conduct the Vedic sacrifice to fulfill his desire for children.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what quality is attributed to Mahavasu-gopa as he undertakes the performance of the Vedic sacrifice?
options: He is described as extremely wealthy
He is described as physically athletic
He is described as a master of music
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly characterizes Mahavasu-gopa as self-controlled when he engages the sage to perform the sacrifice.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what type of religious activity did Mahavasu-gopa commission to attain his desire for children?
options: A grand festival for the gopas
An offering of charity to the brahmanas
questions.explanation: The text notes that Mahavasu-gopa engaged Bhaguri Muni to perform a Vedic sacrifice to achieve his goal of having a heroic son and beautiful daughter.

1.104.verse_text: Certain nectarean foodstuffs appeared from that sacrifice and the delighted Mahavasu gave them to his wife, Sucandra-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what arose from the Vedic sacrifice performed by Bhaguri Muni for Mahavasu-gopa?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that certain nectarean foodstuffs appeared from the sacrifice performed by Bhaguri Muni.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to whom did Mahavasu-gopa give the nectarean foodstuffs after they were manifested from the sacrifice?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the delighted Mahavasu-gopa gave the sacred foodstuffs to his wife, Sucandra-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the emotional state of Mahavasu-gopa when he presented the sacred foodstuffs to his wife?
questions.explanation: The text describes Mahavasu-gopa as being 'delighted' when he gave the foodstuffs to Sucandra-devi.

1.105-106.verse_text: As Sucandra-devi was hastily eating the sacred foodstuff on her front porch, she spilled some of it. At that time the doe named Suranga, who was the mother of the doe named Rangini, was wandering in Vrajabhumi. Seeing Sucandra-devi spill some of the foodstuff, the doe Suranga quickly came forward and ate some of it. As a result of eating this sacred foodstuff, both the gopi Sucandra and the doe Suranga became pregnant.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the name of the doe that wandered into Vrajabhumi and consumed the spilled food from Sucandra-devi?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the doe who ate the sacred food as Suranga, who is also the mother of Rangini.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the doe Suranga is described as being the mother of which specific doe?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Suranga was the mother of the doe named Rangini.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, where was Sucandra-devi positioned when she spilled the sacred foodstuff?
options: Inside the kitchen of Nanda Maharaja
questions.explanation: The text records that Sucandra-devi was eating the food on her front porch when the spilling incident occurred.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the physical consequence for the gopi Sucandra and the doe Suranga after eating the sacred food?
options: They both gained the ability to speak
They both became radiant like gold
questions.explanation: The text states that as a result of eating the sacred food, both the gopi Sucandra and the doe Suranga became pregnant.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what action was Sucandra-devi performing in haste when she spilled the food?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Sucandra-devi was hastily eating the sacred foodstuff on her front porch when she spilled it.

1.107.verse_text: Sucandra-devi gave birth to a son whom the gopas called Stokakrsna and the deer gave birth to the girl Hiranyangi in the village of Vraja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which son is born to the gopi known as Sucandra-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Sucandra-devi gave birth to a son who is known by the gopas as Stokakrsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the daughter born to the doe in the village of Vraja?
questions.explanation: The text states that the doe gave birth to the girl named Hiranyangi within the village of Vraja.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in which specific location is the daughter Hiranyangi born?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the deer gave birth to Hiranyangi in the village of Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what name do the gopas call the son of Sucandra-devi?
questions.explanation: The text confirms that the gopas call the son of Sucandra-devi by the name Stokakrsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the mother of the girl known as Hiranyangi?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the mother of Hiranyangi simply as the deer in the context of the village of Vraja.

1.108.verse_text: Hiranyangi is very dear to Srimati Radharani and Srimati Radharani is very dear to her. Hiranyangi is dressed in beautiful garments that appear like a great multitude of unparalleled blossoming flowers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the mutual affection between Srimati Radharani and Hiranyangi described?
options: They are indifferent toward one another
They are both very dear to each other
questions.correct_answer: They are both very dear to each other
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Hiranyangi is very dear to Srimati Radharani and Srimati Radharani is very dear to her.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the appearance of the garments worn by Hiranyangi?
options: They appear like a golden sunset
They appear like a great multitude of unparalleled blossoming flowers
They appear like the dark blue clouds of autumn
They appear like the shining rays of the full moon
questions.correct_answer: They appear like a great multitude of unparalleled blossoming flowers
questions.explanation: The text describes Hiranyangi as being dressed in beautiful garments that appear like a great multitude of unparalleled blossoming flowers.

1.109.verse_text: With great respect the father Mahavasu then promised the exalted Hiranyangi in marriage to an elderly gopa who was completely unfit to become her husband.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the father who promised Hiranyangi in marriage to an elderly gopa?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Mahavasu is the father who promised Hiranyangi in marriage.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what kind of individual was Hiranyangi promised to in marriage by her father?
questions.explanation: The text notes that Hiranyangi's father promised her to an elderly gopa who was completely unfit to be her husband.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what quality is attributed to the elderly gopa whom Mahavasu selected for Hiranyangi?
options: He was a close friend of Nanda Maharaja
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that the elderly gopa chosen by Mahavasu was completely unfit to become Hiranyangi's husband.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, with what attitude did Mahavasu promise Hiranyangi in marriage?
questions.explanation: The text states that Mahavasu promised Hiranyangi to the elderly gopa with great respect.

1.110-111.verse_text: Ratnalekha: Maharaj Vrsabhanu's maternal cousin Payonidhi had a son but no daughter. His wife Mitra-devi desired to have a daughter also and to achieve this end she faithfully worshiped the sun-god, Vivasvan. By Vivasvan's mercy she gave birth to a daughter who was named Ratnalekha.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the mother of the gopi named Ratnalekha?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Payonidhi's wife, Mitra-devi, worshiped the sun-god and gave birth to Ratnalekha.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to which deity did Mitra-devi offer her faithful worship to be blessed with a daughter?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Mitra-devi faithfully worshiped the sun-god, Vivasvan, to achieve her desire of having a daughter.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the familial relationship between Maharaja Vrsabhanu and Payonidhi?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Payonidhi specifically as the maternal cousin of Maharaja Vrsabhanu.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the specific outcome regarding the children of Payonidhi before the birth of Ratnalekha?
options: He had a son but no daughter
He had two daughters and no sons
He had no children at all
He had a daughter but no son
questions.correct_answer: He had a son but no daughter
questions.explanation: The scripture notes that Payonidhi previously had a son, but lacked a daughter, which led his wife to perform worship.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the daughter born to the wife of Payonidhi?
questions.explanation: The text clearly states that by the mercy of Vivasvan, the daughter born to Mitra-devi was named Ratnalekha.

1.112.verse_text: Ratnalekha's complexion is the red color of the mineral manahsila and her garments appear like a beautiful swarm of bumblebees. She is very dear to Srimati Radharani, the daughter of Maharaja Vrsabhanu. Her mother engages both her and her friend Radharani in the devoted attentive worship of the sun-god. When Ratnalekha sees Lord Madhava, her eyes begin to roll in ferocious anger and she severly rebukes Him.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of Ratnalekha?
options: The yellow color of a karnikara flower
The dark blue color of a monsoon cloud
The fair color of a champaka flower
questions.explanation: Ratnalekha's complexion is explicitly described as having the red hue of the mineral manahsila.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to what is the appearance of Ratnalekha's garments compared?
options: The rays of the rising sun
The petals of a lotus flower
A cluster of fresh monsoon clouds
questions.explanation: The text states that Ratnalekha's garments appear like a beautiful swarm of bumblebees.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whom does Ratnalekha's mother engage in the worship of the sun-god?
questions.explanation: Ratnalekha's mother engages both Ratnalekha and her friend Radharani in the attentive worship of the sun-god.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the reaction of Ratnalekha when she sees Lord Madhava?
options: Her eyes roll in ferocious anger and she rebukes Him
She hides behind a tree in shyness
She offers Him a garland of flowers
She remains silent and bows her head
questions.correct_answer: Her eyes roll in ferocious anger and she rebukes Him
questions.explanation: The text describes that when Ratnalekha sees Lord Madhava, her eyes roll in ferocious anger and she severely rebukes Him.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which Srimati Radharani is described as being very dear to Ratnalekha?
questions.explanation: Ratnalekha is described as being very dear to Srimati Radharani, the daughter of Maharaja Vrsabhanu.

1.113.verse_text: Sikhavati: Sikhavati's father is Dhanyadhanya and her mother is Susikha-devi. Sikhavati's complexion is the color of a karnikara flower. She is the younger sister of Kundalatika.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the father of the gopi known as Sikhavati?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Sikhavati's father is Dhanyadhanya.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the mother of the lovely gopi Sikhavati?
questions.explanation: According to the description provided, Sikhavati's mother is Susikha-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which flower is used to describe the radiant complexion of Sikhavati?
questions.explanation: The scripture describes Sikhavati's complexion as being the color of a karnikara flower.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the elder sister of the gopi Sikhavati?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sikhavati as being the younger sister of Kundalatika.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the familial relationship between Sikhavati and Kundalatika defined?
options: Sikhavati is the younger sister of Kundalatika
Sikhavati is the mother of Kundalatika
Sikhavati is the elder sister of Kundalatika
Sikhavati is the aunt of Kundalatika
questions.correct_answer: Sikhavati is the younger sister of Kundalatika
questions.explanation: The text specifically mentions that Sikhavati is the younger sister of Kundalatika.

1.114.verse_text: She is like sweetness and charm personified. Her garments are the spotted color of an old francoline partridge. She is married to Garjara-gopa, who is also known as Garuda-gopa.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the gopi who is described as sweetness and charm personified wears garments of what specific color pattern?
options: The spotted color of an old francoline partridge
The deep yellow color of a champaka flower
The brilliant crimson of a rising sun
The shimmering blue of a peacock feather
questions.correct_answer: The spotted color of an old francoline partridge
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that her garments are the spotted color of an old francoline partridge.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to whom is the gopi who possesses the beauty of sweetness and charm personified married?
questions.explanation: The text states she is married to Garjara-gopa, who is also known as Garuda-gopa.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what other name is Garjara-gopa, the husband of the sweet gopi, known?
questions.explanation: The text provides that Garjara-gopa is also known as Garuda-gopa.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two qualities are personified in the gopi who is married to Garjara-gopa?
questions.explanation: The verse begins by describing her as sweetness and charm personified.

1.115-116.verse_text: Kandarpa-manjari: Kandarpa-manjari's father is Puspakara and her mother is Kuruvinda-devi. Kandarpa-manjari's father did not give her marriage to anyone, for he considered in his heart that Lord Hari is the only suitable husband for his daughter. Kandarpa-manjari's complexion is the color of a kinkirata bird and her garments are decorated with many different colors.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the mother of the gopi named Kandarpa-manjari?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Kandarpa-manjari's mother is Kuruvinda-devi.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Kandarpa-manjari's father?
questions.explanation: The text states that Kandarpa-manjari's father is Puspakara.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why did Kandarpa-manjari's father refrain from giving her in marriage to anyone?
options: He considered Lord Hari to be the only suitable husband for her
She had taken a lifelong vow of celibacy
He was waiting for a sign from Nanda Maharaja
She was already secretly betrothed to Krishna
questions.correct_answer: He considered Lord Hari to be the only suitable husband for her
questions.explanation: The text notes that her father did not arrange her marriage because he considered Lord Hari the only suitable husband for her.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of Kandarpa-manjari's complexion?
options: The color of a kinkirata bird
Dark like a blue lotus flower
The spotted color of an old francoline partridge
questions.correct_answer: The color of a kinkirata bird
questions.explanation: The text describes Kandarpa-manjari's complexion as the color of a kinkirata bird.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the garments of Kandarpa-manjari described?
options: Like the color of a rainbow
Pure white like a blooming jasmine
questions.explanation: The text describes that Kandarpa-manjari's garments are decorated with many different colors.

1.117.verse_text: Phullakalika: Phullakalika's father is Srimalla and her mother is Kamalini. Phullakalika's complexion is dark like a blue lotus flower and her garments are like a rainbow.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the father of the gopi Phullakalika?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Srimalla as the father of Phullakalika.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of Phullakalika's mother?
questions.explanation: Phullakalika is stated to be the daughter of Srimalla and Kamalini.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to which flower is the complexion of Phullakalika compared?
questions.explanation: Phullakalika's complexion is described as being dark like a blue lotus flower.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which natural phenomenon is used to describe the appearance of Phullakalika's garments?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that Phullakalika's garments are decorated in a way that makes them resemble a rainbow.

1.118.verse_text: Her forehead is naturally marked with yellow tilaka lines. Her husband, Vidura has a loud voice and is able to call the buffaloes from a great distance.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the characteristic markings found on the forehead of the sakhi described in this verse?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that her forehead is naturally marked with yellow tilaka lines.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the husband of the gopi possessing the loud-voiced buffalo caller?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Vidura as the husband of the individual described, noting his ability to call buffaloes from a great distance.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the special talent or ability of Vidura, the husband mentioned in this description?
options: Playing the murali with divine sweetness
Calling buffaloes from a great distance
Leading the gopas into the forest
questions.correct_answer: Calling buffaloes from a great distance
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Vidura has a loud voice and is capable of calling the buffaloes from a great distance.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the voice of Vidura characterized in the description of his service?
questions.explanation: The text describes Vidura as having a loud voice, which allows him to perform his duty of calling buffaloes.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the nature of the tilaka markings on the forehead of the lady mentioned in the text?
options: They are artificially applied by her friends
They are remnants from the morning worship
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that her forehead is naturally marked with yellow tilaka lines.

1.119.verse_text: Ananga-manjari: Ananga-manjari is exquistely beautiful and therefore it is very appropriate that she is named after Ananga (cupid). Her complexion is the color of a springtime ketaki flower and her garments are the color of a blue lotus.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific complexion of Ananga-manjari?
options: The color of a springtime ketaki flower
The color of a fresh monsoon cloud
The color of a blooming mallika flower
questions.correct_answer: The color of a springtime ketaki flower
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Ananga-manjari's complexion is the color of a springtime ketaki flower.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the color of the garments worn by the exquisitely beautiful Ananga-manjari?
options: The color of a blue lotus
The color of a lightning bolt
The color of a sunset sky
The color of a peacock feather
questions.correct_answer: The color of a blue lotus
questions.explanation: The description identifies her garments as being the color of a blue lotus.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why is it considered appropriate that Ananga-manjari bears her specific name?
options: Because she is very dear to Lalita
Because she resides in a garden of flowers
Because she possesses the power of attraction
questions.explanation: The verse notes that she is exquisitely beautiful, making the name Ananga (Cupid) highly appropriate for her.

1.120.verse_text: Her proud husband Durmada is also her sister's brother-in-law. She is especially dear to Lalita and Visakha.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the name of the husband of Ananga-manjari?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Ananga-manjari's proud husband is named Durmada.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific relationship of Durmada to Ananga-manjari's sister?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Durmada as the brother-in-law of Ananga-manjari's sister.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the two prominent gopis to whom Ananga-manjari is especially dear?
questions.explanation: The text notes that Ananga-manjari holds a position of being especially dear to both Lalita and Visakha.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the nature of Ananga-manjari's husband, Durmada, described?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly refers to her husband Durmada using the adjective 'proud'.

1.121.verse_text: General Description of the Activities of Krsna's Contemporary Gopi Friends: Krsna's contemporary gopi friends decorate their own dear friend Srimati Radharani. They cheat their husbands parents and other guardians (by going to meet Radha and Krsna) and they take Srimati Radharani's side when she has a lover's quarrel with Lord Hari.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary service performed by the contemporary gopi friends of Krishna toward Srimati Radharani?
options: Adorning her hair with forest flowers
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krishna's contemporary gopi friends decorate their own dear friend Srimati Radharani.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what do the gopi friends do regarding their own husbands, parents, and guardians to facilitate their devotional service?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that these gopis cheat their husbands, parents, and other guardians to meet Radha and Krishna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which side do the gopi friends take during a lover's quarrel between Radha and Lord Hari?
options: They take the side of their husbands
questions.explanation: The text specifies that the gopis take Srimati Radharani's side when she has a lover's quarrel with Lord Hari.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific personality is referred to as 'Lord Hari' in the context of the gopis' behavior during a quarrel?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the divine figure in the lover's quarrel with Srimati Radharani as Lord Hari.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what acts as the catalyst for the gopis to take sides in a conflict between the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The text notes that the gopis take Radha's side specifically when she has a lover's quarrel with Lord Hari.

1.122.verse_text: These gopis assist Radha and Krsna in Their secret rendezvous. These gopis serve palatable meals to the divine couple and afterward relish tasting the remnants left by Them. These gopis carefully guard the secret of Radha's and Krsna's confidential pastimes together.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary role of the gopi friends regarding the confidential activities of Radha and Krishna?
options: They assist the divine couple in their secret rendezvous
They offer elaborate prayers for the couple's long life
They decorate the forest bowers with fresh flowers
They sing songs of separation to increase the couple's longing
questions.correct_answer: They assist the divine couple in their secret rendezvous
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that these gopis assist Radha and Krishna in their secret rendezvous.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, after the gopi friends serve palatable meals to the divine couple, what is their next action?
options: They clean the dining area with aromatic water
They relish tasting the remnants left by Radha and Krishna
They offer flowers to the divine feet of the couple
They arrange for the couple to rest in a private bedchamber
questions.correct_answer: They relish tasting the remnants left by Radha and Krishna
questions.explanation: The text mentions that after serving palatable meals, they relish tasting the remnants left by them.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what duty do the gopi friends perform regarding the confidential affairs of the divine couple?
options: They spread news of the meetings to other gopis
They carefully guard the secret of the couple's confidential pastimes
They write poetry describing the beauty of the rendezvous
They decorate the path the couple takes to their meeting spot
questions.correct_answer: They carefully guard the secret of the couple's confidential pastimes
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that these gopis carefully guard the secret of Radha's and Krishna's confidential pastimes together.

1.123.verse_text: Their minds are pure and they are very expert and intelligent. They serve Radha and Krsna very appropriately. They glorify their own group and criticize the followers of Candravali, Radharani's rival.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the two primary mental qualities mentioned for the gopis who serve the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the gopis' minds are pure and that they are very expert and intelligent in their service.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is one of the notable behaviors of these gopis regarding their own group?
options: They remain silent about their service
They perform austerities in the forest
They serve meals to the cowherd boys
questions.explanation: The text describes that they glorify their own group while simultaneously criticizing the followers of Candravali.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which rival's followers are specifically criticized by the group of gopis described?
questions.explanation: The text clearly identifies Candravali as the rival whose followers are the target of criticism by these gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what third intellectual trait, alongside purity and expertise, characterizes these gopis?
questions.explanation: The verse states their minds are pure, they are very expert, and they are very intelligent.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how do the gopis approach the act of serving Radha and Krishna?
options: They serve only from a distance
questions.explanation: The text specifies that these gopis serve Radha and Krsna very appropriately, reflecting their expert nature.

1.124.verse_text: They please Radha and Krsna by expertly singing, dancing and playing instrumental music. At the appropriate time they beg to be engaged in various appropriate services.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the three specific artistic performances mentioned that the gopis use to please Radha and Krsna?
options: Singing, dancing, and playing instrumental music
Tying garlands, decorating, and painting feet
questions.correct_answer: Singing, dancing, and playing instrumental music
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that they please Radha and Krsna by expertly singing, dancing, and playing instrumental music.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, when do the gopis approach Radha and Krsna to request engagement in various services?
questions.explanation: The text states that at the appropriate time, the gopis beg to be engaged in various appropriate services.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how do the gopis describe their state of mind while engaging in their devotional services to Radha and Krsna?
questions.explanation: While the immediate verse focuses on their actions, it follows the description that they are expert and intelligent.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary motive behind the gopis performing singing, dancing, and playing music?
options: To earn praise from the residents of Vraja
To surpass the followers of Candravali
questions.explanation: The text clearly mentions that the purpose of their artistic performances is to please Radha and Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how do the gopis actively pursue their roles in the pastimes of the Divine Couple?
options: By waiting to be called by Krsna
By begging to be engaged in various appropriate services
By performing duties only when observed by others
By observing the activities of the cowherd boys
questions.correct_answer: By begging to be engaged in various appropriate services
questions.explanation: The verse notes that at the appropriate time, the gopis beg to be engaged in various appropriate services.

1.125.verse_text: For the most part the learned devotees know about these activities of Krsna's gopi friends. All the gopis are aware of these and all the other similar services and they actively serve Radha and Krsna by performing these activities.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to what extent are the gopi friends of Krsna familiar with the varied services performed for the Divine Couple?
options: Only the principal eight gopis are fully aware
Most learned devotees and all the gopis are aware of these activities
Only those specifically initiated into secret service know
The knowledge is restricted to the elder gopis of Vraja
questions.correct_answer: Most learned devotees and all the gopis are aware of these activities
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that for the most part, learned devotees know of these activities, and all the gopis are aware of these and other similar services.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary engagement of the gopis regarding the activities described for the pleasure of Radha and Krsna?
options: They observe from a distance to maintain etiquette
They actively serve Radha and Krsna by performing these activities
