questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in which dynasty was Suprasada-devi born?
questions.explanation: The text states that Suprasada-devi was born in the Puru dynasty.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the gopi mentioned as coming from a family of ascetics?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sadasanta-devi as having been born in a family of ascetics.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two gopis were born in brahmana families?
questions.explanation: The text specifies that Santida and Kantida were born in brahmana families.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose mercy allowed these specific gopis to reside in Vraja?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that by the mercy of Narada Muni they were all able to reside in Vraja.

1.230.verse_text: The Second Gruop of Gopis: After this first group of gopis there is a second group of gopis, whose love for the divine couple is a little less than that of the first group. This second group of gopis may again be divided into two groups : sama-prema and asama-prema. These gopis will be described in the following verses.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the intensity of the love of the second group of gopis described in comparison to the first group?
options: It is exactly equal to the first group
It is significantly greater than the first group
It is a little less than that of the first group
It is completely devoid of prema
questions.correct_answer: It is a little less than that of the first group
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the love for the divine couple of the second group of gopis is a little less than that of the first group.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the two specific sub-categories into which the second group of gopis is divided?
questions.explanation: The text states that the second group of gopis may be divided into two groups: sama-prema and asama-prema.

1.231.verse_text: The sama-prema gopis may be divided into two groups - those who are eternally perfect and those who have attained perfection by engaging in devotional service.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the sama-prema gopis are categorized into how many distinct groups based on their attainment of perfection?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the sama-prema gopis are divided into two groups: those eternally perfect and those who attained perfection through devotional service.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the two specific classifications of the sama-prema gopis regarding their state of perfection?
options: Eternally perfect and those who attained perfection by devotional service
Born in Vraja and those who moved to Vraja
Those who serve the Queen and those who serve the King
questions.correct_answer: Eternally perfect and those who attained perfection by devotional service
questions.explanation: The text describes the two divisions of the sama-prema gopis as those who are nitya-siddha (eternally perfect) and those who have achieved perfection through the practice of devotional service.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which classification of gopis is specifically divided into those who are eternally perfect and those who attained perfection by devotional service?
options: The gopis of the first group
The gopis of the highest intensity of love
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the subdivision into eternally perfect and sadhana-siddha refers specifically to the sama-prema category of gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, one of the two groups of sama-prema gopis is composed of those who have attained perfection through what means?
options: Performing severe austerities in the forest
questions.explanation: The text defines the second group of the sama-prema gopis as those who attained perfection specifically by engaging in devotional service.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group of gopis, beyond the first group of higher love, is the subject of the structural division into eternally perfect and perfection-attained states?
options: The gopis of the third rank
The gopis who live in the outskirts of Vraja
questions.explanation: The text focuses the structural division of eternally perfect and perfection-attained status on the sama-prema group, which follows the first group mentioned in the preceding context.

1.232.verse_text: Out of one hundred million gopis, eight hundred thousand are eternally perfect.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, out of the total population of one hundred million gopis, how many are described as being eternally perfect?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that among one hundred million gopis, eight hundred thousand are eternally perfect.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the total number of gopis mentioned when considering the subset of eight hundred thousand eternally perfect ones?
questions.explanation: The verse establishes that the eternally perfect gopis numbering eight hundred thousand are counted out of an original group of one hundred million.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which classification of gopis are identified as numbering eight hundred thousand?
options: The gopis who attained perfection through sadhana
The gopis who are direct followers of the eight principal gopis
The gopis who live in the outskirts of Vrindavana
questions.explanation: The text defines that out of the one hundred million gopis, the eternally perfect ones specifically number eight hundred thousand.

1.233-234.verse_text: The direct followers of the eight principal gopis are counted in different ways. Some say they number five thousand, others say they number four or five thousand, still others say they number three or four thousand and still others say they number one thousand.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the varying counts attributed to the number of direct followers belonging to the eight principal gopis?
options: One, two, three, or four thousand
Five, four, three, or one thousand
Eight, six, four, or two thousand
Ten, eight, five, or three thousand
questions.correct_answer: Five, four, three, or one thousand
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions these four different counts as being attributed to the followers of the eight principal gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many direct followers are estimated to accompany each of the eight principal gopis according to the various traditional accounts mentioned?
options: One, three, four, or five thousand
questions.correct_answer: One, three, four, or five thousand
questions.explanation: The verse lists these specific numbers as the diverse estimates given by different authorities regarding the followers of the eight principal gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, when discussing the direct followers of the eight principal gopis, what is the smallest number mentioned among the varying estimates?
questions.explanation: The text lists one thousand as the minimum figure among the different counts provided for the followers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the largest number mentioned when describing the count of the direct followers of the eight principal gopis?
questions.explanation: The range of counts provided in the text culminates at five thousand as the upper limit.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, regarding the direct followers of the eight principal gopis, how many estimates of their total number are recorded in this specific passage?
questions.explanation: The verse lists four specific variations: five thousand, four thousand, three thousand, and one thousand.

1.235.verse_text: Some say the followers of the eight principal gopis are divided into many groups and others say they are not divided into groups at all. Some say they are divided into sixteen groups according to the nature of their love for the divine couple.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many distinct groups are some authorities said to recognize among the followers of the eight principal gopis based on the specific nature of their love for the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that some say the followers are divided into sixteen groups according to the nature of their love for the Divine Couple.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the varying opinions regarding the organizational structure of the eight principal gopis' followers?
options: They are either divided into many groups or not divided into groups at all
They are divided exclusively by age or by birthplace
They are divided solely by their service roles or by their complexion
They are divided into either one thousand or five thousand permanent clans
questions.correct_answer: They are either divided into many groups or not divided into groups at all
questions.explanation: The verse states that some say the followers are divided into many groups while others maintain they are not divided into groups at all.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the basis for the potential division of the gopis into sixteen specific groups?
options: The nature of their love for the Divine Couple
Their varied ages and stages of youth
The specific services they perform for Srimati Radharani
questions.correct_answer: The nature of their love for the Divine Couple
questions.explanation: The text specifies that the division into sixteen groups is based upon the nature of the gopis' love for the Divine Couple.

1.236.verse_text: Some say these gopis are divided into twenty groups, some say there are twenty-five groups, some say there are thirty, some say there are sixty and some say there are sixty-four groups of gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the maximum number of groups into which the gopis are said to be divided, according to one of the traditional accounts mentioned?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly lists various schools of thought regarding the classification of gopi groups, concluding with the number sixty-four.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is cited as a possible number of gopi groups alongside twenty, twenty-five, and sixty-four?
questions.explanation: The verse lists twenty, twenty-five, thirty, sixty, and sixty-four as the various numbers of groups suggested by different authorities.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many distinct group counts for the gopis are presented as scholarly perspectives?
questions.explanation: The text enumerates five specific estimations: twenty, twenty-five, thirty, sixty, and sixty-four.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which number is mentioned as a potential grouping count for the gopis immediately following the mention of thirty?
questions.explanation: The sequence provided in the text identifies twenty, twenty-five, thirty, and then sixty as proposed counts for the gopi groups.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the smallest number mentioned among the various groupings of the gopis?
questions.explanation: Among the options presented in the text regarding the divisions of gopis, twenty is the lowest count mentioned.

1.237.verse_text: Some say this group of gopis is in turn divided into two subgroups. Others say it is divided into two or three subgroups and others say it is divided into three or four subgroups.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the varying opinions regarding the number of subgroups into which the mentioned group of gopis can be divided?
options: Two, two or three, or three or four
Three, three or four, or four or five
questions.correct_answer: Two, two or three, or three or four
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly lists these three distinct perspectives on the potential division of these specific gopi groups.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the classification of two or three subgroups, what is the third configuration mentioned for this specific group of gopis?
questions.explanation: The text clearly delineates the division opinions as being either two, two or three, or three or four.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, when discussing the internal divisions of a specific group of gopis, how many different grouping configurations are cited as being proposed by others?
questions.explanation: The verse provides three specific variations regarding how the gopis are subdivided.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many subgroups are mentioned as the smallest possible division for the gopi group being described?
questions.explanation: The text indicates the smallest subdivision mentioned is two subgroups.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is NOT listed as a potential division for the gopi groups described?
questions.explanation: The text mentions 'two', 'two or three', and 'three or four' as the possible subgroup divisions, but does not include 'four or five'.

1.238.verse_text: The entire gopi community may be divided in different ways. Some say there are forty groups of gopis and others that there are five hundred groups.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the two specific counts mentioned for the total number of groups within the gopi community?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that some opinions suggest forty groups while others suggest five hundred groups of gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the internal structure of the gopi community described?
options: It may be divided in different ways
It is strictly divided into eight primary groups
It is fixed into a single hierarchy of leaders
questions.correct_answer: It may be divided in different ways
questions.explanation: The text acknowledges that the entire gopi community can be categorized or divided in various different ways.

1.239-246.verse_text: Aside from the eight varistha-gopas and the eight vara-gopis, sixty four gopis are considered most important. Their names are : 1. Ratnaprabha, 2. Ratikala, 3. Subhadra, 4. Ratika, 5. Sumukhi, 6. Dhanistha, 7. Kalahamsi, 8. Kalapini, 9. Madhavi, 10. Malati, 11. Candrarekha, 12. Kunjari, 13. Harini, 14. Capala, 15. Damni, 16. Surabhi, 17. Subhanana, 18. Kurangaksi, 19. Sucarita, 20. Mandali, 21. Manikundala, 22. Candrika, 23. Candralatika, 24. Pankajaksi, 25. Sumandira, 26. Rasalika, 27. Tilakini, 28. Sauraseni, 29. Sugandhika, 30. Ramani, 31. Kamanagari, 32. Nagari, 33. Nagavenika, 34. Manjumedha, 35. Sumadhura, 36. Sumadhya, 37. Madhureksana, 38. Tanumadhya, 39. Madhuspanda, 40. Gunacuda, 41. Varangada, 42. Tungabhadra, 43. Rasottunga, 44. Rangavati, 45. Susangata, 46. Citrarekha, 47. Vicitrangi, 48. Modini, 49. Madanalasa, 50. Kalakanthi, 51. Sasikala, 52. Kamala, 53. Madhurendira, 54. Kandarpa-sundari, 55. Kamalatika, 56. Prema-manjari, 57. Kaveri, 58. Carukavara, 59. Sukesi, 60. Manjukesi, 61. Harahira, 62. Mahahira, 63. Harakanthi, 64. Manohara.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides the eight varistha-gopas and the eight vara-gopis, how many gopis are specifically identified as the most important?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that aside from the eight varistha-gopas and eight vara-gopis, sixty four gopis are considered most important.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these exalted gopis appears at the beginning of the list of the sixty four most important gopis?
questions.explanation: Ratnaprabha is listed as the first name in the enumeration of the sixty four most important gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which name concludes the list of the sixty four most important gopis?
questions.explanation: Manohara is the sixty-fourth and final name mentioned in the list of the sixty four most important gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is included in the list of the sixty four most important gopis?
questions.explanation: Prema-manjari is specifically enumerated as one of the sixty four most important gopis in the text.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these is counted among the sixty four most important gopis?
questions.explanation: Tungabhadra is listed as the forty-second gopi in the group of the sixty four most important gopis.

1.247.verse_text: The Sammohana-tantra's Description of Srimati Radharani's Eight Principal Friends: "Lilavati, Sadhika, Candrika, Madhavi, Lalita, Vijaya, Gauri and Nandi are the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani."

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani are listed in the excerpt from the Sammohana-tantra?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states there are eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani mentioned in the Sammohana-tantra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is included among the eight principal gopi friends of Srimati Radharani as identified in the Sammohana-tantra?
questions.explanation: Lalita is specifically named as one of the eight principal gopi friends in the verse citation from the Sammohana-tantra.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which gopi from the following list is mentioned alongside Lilavati as a principal friend of Srimati Radharani?
questions.explanation: Sadhika is listed immediately following Lilavati in the Sammohana-tantra's description of Srimati Radharani's eight principal gopi friends.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following gopis is listed in the Sammohana-tantra passage as one of Srimati Radharani's principal friends?
questions.explanation: Madhavi is explicitly mentioned as one of the eight principal friends of Srimati Radharani in the Sammohana-tantra citation.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which scripture is the source for the names Lilavati, Sadhika, Candrika, Madhavi, Lalita, Vijaya, Gauri, and Nandi?
questions.explanation: The text clearly attributes the list of these eight principal gopi friends to the Sammohana-tantra.

1.248.verse_text: Another Description of the Eight Principal Gopis: Another quotation from Vedic literature gives the following description : "Kalavati, Srimati, Sudhamukhi, Visakha, Kaumudi, Madhvi and Sarada are the eight prinicpal gopis."

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the eight principal gopis in the quoted Vedic description?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly names Kalavati, Srimati, Sudhamukhi, Visakha, Kaumudi, Madhvi, and Sarada as the eight principal gopis mentioned in this specific Vedic quotation.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which divine associate is mentioned alongside Kalavati in the list of eight principal gopis?
questions.explanation: The text lists Srimati as one of the eight principal gopis alongside Kalavati, Sudhamukhi, Visakha, Kaumudi, Madhvi, and Sarada.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names is included in the specific Vedic list of the eight principal gopis?
questions.explanation: Sudhamukhi is explicitly identified in the text as one of the eight principal gopis belonging to the quoted Vedic description.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which gopi is mentioned as part of the eight principal gopis, distinct from the group mentioned in the Sammohana-tantra?
questions.explanation: Kaumudi is one of the eight gopis listed in the Vedic quotation provided in the text, distinguishing her from the list found in the Sammohana-tantra.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the eight principal gopis in the Vedic passage provided?
questions.explanation: The verse concludes its list of the eight principal gopis by including Sarada along with Kalavati, Srimati, Sudhamukhi, Visakha, Kaumudi, and Madhvi.

1.249-250.verse_text: The numbers presented in this description of the gopis has no actual relevance to the number of eternally liberated gopis in the spiritual world. There the associates of the king and queen of Vrndavana are unlimited in number. No one can count them. We have simply given some hints so the reader may understand the vast number of the associates of the divine couple.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is stated regarding the count of eternally liberated gopis in the spiritual world?
options: They number exactly one hundred and eight
They are counted by the Vedas as ten thousand
They are numbered according to the desire of the yogis
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that the number of eternally liberated gopis in the spiritual realm is unlimited and cannot be counted.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, why are specific lists or numbers of gopis provided in the text?
options: To satisfy the curiosity of the neophyte students
To define the exact boundaries of the Lord's service
To give the reader hints about the vast number of the Divine Couple's associates
To limit the number of gopis to those mentioned in the Vedas
questions.correct_answer: To give the reader hints about the vast number of the Divine Couple's associates
questions.explanation: The author explains that the numbers given are simply to help the reader understand the vastness of the associates of Radha and Krishna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the ability of anyone to count the gopis described?
options: It is possible only for the great sages of the Vedas
It can be done through deep meditation
Only Srimati Radharani can count them
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that no one possesses the capacity to count the unlimited associates of the King and Queen of Vrndavana.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the status of the numbers presented in the description of the gopis?
options: They represent the final absolute tally
They have no actual relevance to the number of eternally liberated gopis
They are the exact number of gopis serving in the morning lila
They are the secret numbers known only to the sakhis
questions.correct_answer: They have no actual relevance to the number of eternally liberated gopis
questions.explanation: The text clarifies that the numbers provided in the description do not reflect the actual limit or totality of the eternally liberated associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are described as the 'King and Queen' of Vrndavana?
questions.explanation: The text refers to the Divine Couple, Radha and Krsna, as the King and Queen of Vrndavana.

1.251.verse_text: Preparing the divine couple's couch, food, drink and betel nuts, swinging Them on a swing and decorating Them with tilaka are among the many services performed by the gopis. The learned devotees may research this and enumerate all these services.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is specifically mentioned as a service performed by the gopis for the Divine Couple?
options: Washing the lotus feet of the cows
Herding the calves in the pasture
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly lists preparing the couch, food, drink, and betel nuts among the services performed by the gopis for the Divine Couple.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which item among the listed offerings are the gopis described as preparing for the enjoyment of the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the preparation of food, drink, and betel nuts as specific services rendered by the gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what artistic service do the gopis perform regarding the bodily adornment of the Divine Couple?
questions.explanation: The verse mentions decorating the Divine Couple with tilaka as one of the many services performed by the gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what playful activity do the gopis engage the Divine Couple in as part of their dedicated service?
options: Rowing a boat on the Yamuna
questions.explanation: The text lists swinging the Divine Couple on a swing as one of the services performed by the gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which duty is listed alongside the preparation of food and drink as a service of the gopis?
questions.explanation: The verse lists the couch, food, drink, and betel nuts as essential items the gopis prepare for the Divine Couple.

1.252.verse_text: Rupa Goswami prays that the effulgent Krsna-sun may remove the blindness from his eyes and enable him to properly see and understand the various rasa-sastras that describe Sri Sri Radha-Krsna and Their associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to what celestial body does Srila Rupa Gosvami compare Lord Krishna when praying for the removal of his own blindness?
questions.explanation: Srila Rupa Gosvami refers to the effulgent Krishna-sun to illuminate his vision and understanding.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specifically does Srila Rupa Gosvami desire to see and understand clearly through his prayer?
options: The specific ornaments of the Gopis
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states his desire to see and understand the various rasa-sastras describing Radha-Krishna and Their associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what obstacle does Srila Rupa Gosvami ask Lord Krishna to remove from his eyes?
questions.explanation: Rupa Gosvami prays that the effulgent Krishna-sun may remove the blindness from his eyes.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, whose pastimes and associations are the subject of the rasa-sastras that the author wishes to understand?
questions.explanation: The text confirms the rasa-sastras in question describe Sri Sri Radha-Krishna and Their associates.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the prerequisite for Srila Rupa Gosvami to properly perceive the divine associates of the Lord?
options: Having his blindness removed by the Krishna-sun
Traveling to the holy land of Nandagrama
Completing the work in the month of Sravana
Studying under the guidance of the Gosvamis
questions.correct_answer: Having his blindness removed by the Krishna-sun
questions.explanation: The author prays for the removal of his blindness by the Krishna-sun as the essential means to enable him to see and understand the associates.

1.253.verse_text: In the year 1472 of the Saka era (1550 A.D.) on Sunday, the sixth day of the month of Sravana, at Nandagrama, the home of the king of Vraja, this book, the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika was completed.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific location where the completion of this divine text took place?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the Radha-Krsna-ganoddesa-dipika was completed at Nandagrama, the home of the king of Vraja.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in which year of the Saka era was the composition of this work finalized?
questions.explanation: The author records the completion of the book in the year 1472 of the Saka era.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific day of the week mentioned for the completion of this scripture?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sunday as the day on which the work was completed.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, during which lunar month was the text brought to its conclusion?
questions.explanation: The author explicitly mentions the month of Sravana as the time of the book's completion.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific day of the lunar fortnight is noted as the day the writing was finished?
questions.explanation: The verse records that the book was completed on the sixth day of the month of Sravana.

2.1.verse_text: Sri Krsna's Transcendental Form and Attributes: Sri Krsna's form glistens with the black mascara of the sweetness of His nectarean handsomeness. His complexion is the color of a blue lotus flower or a sapphire.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to which gemstone is the complexion of Sri Krishna compared?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly describes Sri Krishna's complexion as being the color of a blue lotus flower or a sapphire.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what substance is metaphorically described as the mascara that makes Sri Krishna's form glisten?
options: The sweetness of His nectarean handsomeness
The dust of the Vraja gopis' lotus feet
The cooling essence of the Yamuna waters
The dark radiance of the monsoon night
questions.correct_answer: The sweetness of His nectarean handsomeness
questions.explanation: The verse states that Sri Krishna's form glistens with the black mascara of the sweetness of His nectarean handsomeness.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which flower is used to describe the hue of Sri Krishna's divine complexion?
questions.explanation: The text compares the complexion of Sri Krishna to a blue lotus flower or a sapphire.

2.2.verse_text: His complexion is as enchanting as an emerald, a tamala tree, or a group of beautiful dark clouds. He is an ocean of nectarean handsomeness.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which gemstone is used to describe the enchanting complexion of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krsna's complexion is as enchanting as an emerald, a tamala tree, or a group of dark clouds.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides emeralds and dark clouds, to what specific tree is Krsna's complexion compared?
questions.explanation: The verse compares the Lord's complexion to a tamala tree, emeralds, and dark clouds.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, Krsna is described as an ocean of what quality?
questions.explanation: The verse specifically identifies Him as an ocean of nectarean handsomeness.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following natural elements is used to metaphorically describe the hue of Krsna's divine form?
questions.explanation: The verse lists beautiful dark clouds as one of the three comparisons for the color of Krsna's complexion.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the enchantingly vibrant complexion of Krsna characterized?
questions.explanation: The verse directly equates the beauty of His complexion to the enchanting quality of an emerald.

2.3.verse_text: He wears yellow garments and a garland of forest flowers. He is decorated with various jewels and He is a great reservoir of the nectar of many transcendental pastimes.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific color of the garments worn by the Supreme Lord?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that He wears yellow garments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what type of garland does the Lord wear upon His divine form?
questions.explanation: The text describes Him as wearing a garland made of forest flowers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides garlands and garments, with what else is the Lord decorated?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that the Lord is decorated with various jewels.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, the Lord is described as a great reservoir of what?
options: The nectar of many transcendental pastimes
The glory of the Vraja residents
The beauty of the autumnal moon
questions.correct_answer: The nectar of many transcendental pastimes
questions.explanation: The text characterizes Him as a great reservoir of the nectar of many transcendental pastimes.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is mentioned as an adornment worn by the Lord?
questions.explanation: The verse specifically identifies a garland of forest flowers as part of His decoration.

2.4.verse_text: He has long, curling hair and He is anointed with many fragrant scents. His handsome crown is decorated with many different flowers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific physical description of the Lord's hair?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that the Lord has long, curling hair.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is applied to the body of the Lord to enhance His transcendental fragrance?
options: A paste of sandalwood and saffron
questions.explanation: The text confirms that He is anointed with many fragrant scents.

To slay the demons of Mathura
To enjoy the company of the beautiful gopis
To play His flute in the forests of Vrindavana

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific color of Krsna's toes?

options: To satisfy the curiosity of scholarly critics
To arouse the attraction of the reader
To document the specific beauty of the Vraja-vasis
To provide a reference for meditation on the deity
questions.correct_answer: To arouse the attraction of the reader
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that this small indication of Krsna's handsomeness is provided to arouse the attraction of the reader.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the author's stance regarding the comparisons made about Krishna's exquisite handsomeness?
options: They are perfect reflections of His beauty
They are insufficient as nothing can actually be equal to it
They are meant to be taken as literal descriptions
They are only applicable to His eternal associates
questions.correct_answer: They are insufficient as nothing can actually be equal to it
questions.explanation: The text acknowledges that although comparisons have been made, nothing can truly equal the handsomeness of Krishna.

2.20.verse_text: Krsna's Friends: Now Lord Krsna's friends will be described. The most important of Krsna's friends is His elder brother, Balarama.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is identified as the most important among all of Lord Krsna's friends?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that the most important of Krsna's friends is His elder brother, Balarama.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific familial relationship between the most important friend of Krsna and Krsna Himself?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Balarama as the most important friend and clarifies that He is Krsna's elder brother.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what major category of associates is introduced immediately following the description of the Lord's handsomeness?
questions.explanation: The verse transitions directly from describing Krsna's handsomeness to the description of His friends.

2.21.verse_text: Different Kinds of Friends: Krsna's friends are divided into four groups : 1. well-wishing friends (suhrt), 2. ordinary friends (sakha), 3. more confidential friends (priya-sakha), and 4. initmate friends (priya-narma-sakha)

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many distinct groups are Lord Krsna’s friends categorized into?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Krsna's friends are divided into four specific groups.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific term used to identify the category of Krsna’s well-wishing friends?

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which classification is given to Krsna’s friends who are described as 'more confidential'?
questions.explanation: The text identifies the third group of friends as the more confidential friends, or priya-sakha.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, by what Sanskrit designation are Krsna’s 'intimate' friends known?
questions.explanation: The fourth and final group of friends mentioned in the text are the intimate friends, referred to as priya-narma-sakha.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the designation given to Krsna’s friends who are simply termed 'ordinary'?
questions.explanation: The second group mentioned in the classification of Krsna’s friends is the ordinary friends, known as sakha.

2.22.verse_text: Well-wishing Friends (suhrt): The well-wishing friends include Krsna's cousins : Subhadra, Kundala, Dandi and Mandala. Sunandana, Nandi, Anandi and others who accompany Krsna as He herds the cows and calves in Vrndavana forest are also well-wishing friends.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of Krsna's cousins among His well-wishing friends?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Subhadra, Kundala, Dandi, and Mandala as the cousins who are Krsna's well-wishing friends.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which companion is mentioned alongside Nandi and Anandi as a well-wishing friend who accompanies Krsna while herding cows?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Sunandana, Nandi, and Anandi as friends who accompany Krsna as He herds the cows and calves in Vrndavana forest.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these individuals is specifically named as a cousin and well-wishing friend of Krsna?
questions.explanation: Dandi is listed by name in the text as one of the cousins categorized as a well-wishing friend (suhrt).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific activity are Krsna's well-wishing friends Sunandana, Nandi, and Anandi engaged in while with Him?

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which name completes the list of Krsna's cousins identified as well-wishing friends: Subhadra, Kundala, Dandi, and ...?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly categorizes Subhadra, Kundala, Dandi, and Mandala as Krsna's cousins who are well-wishing friends.

2.23-24.verse_text: Also included among the well-wishing friends are Subhadra, Mandalibhadra, Bhadravardhana, Gobhata, Yaksendra, Bhata, Bhadranga, Virabhadra, Mahaguna, Kulavira, Mahabhima, Divyasakti, Suraprabha, Ranasthira and others. These well-wishing friends are older than Krsna and they try to protect Him from any danger.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the well-wishing friends who tries to protect Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Subhadra as one of the well-wishing friends included in the list of protectors.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the age relationship between Krsna and the group of well-wishing friends that includes Mandalibhadra and Bhadravardhana?
options: They are all younger than Krsna
They are the same age as Krsna
They are the same age as Balarama
questions.explanation: The text states that these well-wishing friends are older than Krsna and actively try to protect Him.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is identified as one of the well-wishing friends along with Virabhadra and Mahaguna?
questions.explanation: Gobhata is specifically mentioned in the list of well-wishing friends within the target verse.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary duty performed by the group of well-wishing friends that includes Yaksendra, Bhata, and Bhadranga?
options: They herd the calves in the forest
They try to protect Krsna from any danger
They decorate Krsna with forest flowers
They play flutes to entertain Krsna
questions.correct_answer: They try to protect Krsna from any danger
questions.explanation: The verse states that these well-wishing friends try to protect Krsna from any danger.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these individuals is included in the list of well-wishing friends along with Divyasakti and Suraprabha?
questions.explanation: Kulavira is listed as one of the well-wishing friends alongside Divyasakti, Suraprabha, and Ranasthira.

2.25.verse_text: Krsna's parents love their son very dearly. They consider Him many millions of times more important than their own life's breath. Very frightened that the demon Kamsa would harm their son, they engaged these well-wishing friends (suhrt) to protect Him. The leader of these well-wishing friends is a boy named Vijayaksa, whose mother, Ambika-devi, was Krsna's nurse. Ambika-devi worshiped goddess Parvati and performed great austerities to get a powerful son who could protect Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who serves as the leader of the well-wishing friends (suhrt) tasked with protecting young Krsna from Kamsa?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly identifies Vijayaksa as the leader of the well-wishing friends who were engaged to protect Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the name of the woman who served as Krsna's nurse and is the mother of the leader of His well-wishing friends?
questions.explanation: The text states that the leader of the well-wishing friends is Vijayaksa, whose mother, Ambika-devi, was Krsna's nurse.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which deity did Ambika-devi worship and perform great austerities to specifically receive a powerful son capable of protecting Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text mentions that Ambika-devi performed great austerities and worshipped goddess Parvati to be blessed with a son who could protect Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how do Krsna's parents prioritize their affection for their son compared to their own lives?
options: Millions of times more than their own life's breath
Equal to their own life's breath
More than all the wealth of Vraja
As a precious gift from the demigods
questions.correct_answer: Millions of times more than their own life's breath
questions.explanation: The text states that Krsna's parents consider Him many millions of times more important than their own life's breath.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what was the primary motivation that led Krsna's parents to engage the well-wishing friends?
options: Fear that the demon Kamsa would harm Him
To teach Him the arts of cowherding
To help Him manage the cows in the forest
Because of the prophecy of the sages
questions.correct_answer: Fear that the demon Kamsa would harm Him
questions.explanation: The text specifies that because they were very frightened that the demon Kamsa would harm their son, they engaged the well-wishing friends to protect Him.

2.26.verse_text: Subhadra: Subhadra has a splendid dark complexion. He wears yellow garments and various ornaments.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the complexion of the cowherd boy Subhadra?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Subhadra has a splendid dark complexion.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what color of garments is Subhadra described as wearing?
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that Subhadra wears yellow garments.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides his garments, what else does Subhadra wear?
questions.explanation: The verse confirms that Subhadra wears yellow garments and various ornaments.

2.27.verse_text: Subhadra's father is Upananda, and his mother the chaste and faithful Tula-devi. Kundalata-devi will become his wife. Subhadra is full of the glory of youth.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who is the father of Subhadra?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Subhadra's father is Upananda.

questions.explanation: The verse identifies Tula-devi as the mother of Subhadra.

questions.explanation: The text confirms that Kundalata-devi will become the wife of Subhadra.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what quality is described as filling the entirety of Subhadra?
questions.explanation: The text states that Subhadra is full of the glory of youth.

2.28-29.verse_text: Krsna's Ordinary Friends (sakha): Visala, Vrsabha, Ojasvi, Devaprastha, Varuthapa, Mandara, Kusumapida, Manibandhakara, Mandara, Candana, Kunda, Kalindi, Kulika and many others are included in the group of Krsna's ordinary friends (sakha). These friends are younger than Krsna and are always eager to serve Him.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which cowherd friend of Krsna is explicitly mentioned among the group of ordinary friends, or sakhas?
questions.explanation: Visala is listed as one of the ordinary friends of Krsna in the verse describing the group of sakhas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the age of the ordinary friends, such as Vrsabha and Ojasvi, compare to Krsna?
options: They are the exact same age
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that these friends are younger than Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is identified as one of the ordinary friends, or sakhas, of Krsna?
questions.explanation: Kusumapida is specifically mentioned in the list of Krsna's ordinary friends in the text.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary attitude of the ordinary friends, such as Mandara and Candana, towards Krsna?
options: They are always eager to serve Him
They are competitive in wrestling matches
They are deeply concerned with household duties
They are constantly observing religious vows
questions.correct_answer: They are always eager to serve Him
questions.explanation: The verse describes that these friends are always eager to serve Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these devotees is categorized among the group of Krsna's ordinary friends?
questions.explanation: Manibandhakara is listed among the specific names forming the group of Krsna's ordinary friends in the mentioned verses.

2.30-31.verse_text: Krsna's confidential friends (priya-sakha): Krsna's confidential friends are Sridama, Sudama, Dama, Vasudama, Kinkini, Bhadrasena, Amsu, Stokakrsna, Vilasi, Pundarika, Vitankaksa, Kalavinka and Priyaskara. These friends are the smae age of Krsna. Their leader is Sridama, who is also known as Pithamardaka.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which confidential friend of Krsna is famously known by the name Pithamardaka?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Sridama is the leader of the confidential friends and is also known as Pithamardaka.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the age of Krsna's confidential friends (priya-sakha) compare to the age of Krsna Himself?
options: They are the same age as Krsna
They are infinitely older than Krsna
questions.correct_answer: They are the same age as Krsna
questions.explanation: The verse clarifies that the confidential friends (priya-sakha) are the same age as Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of Krsna's confidential friends?
questions.explanation: Kalavinka is explicitly mentioned in the list of Krsna's confidential friends provided in the text.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which confidential friend of Krsna is included in the list alongside Pundarika and Vitankaksa?
questions.explanation: Vilasi is specifically identified as one of the confidential friends (priya-sakha) of Krsna in the list provided.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who serves as the designated leader of all the confidential friends (priya-sakha)?
questions.explanation: The text states that Sridama is the leader of the confidential friends of Krsna.

2.32.verse_text: Bhadrasena is the general who leads Krsna's childhood friends in military pastimes. Stokakrsna is very appropriately named, for he is just like a small (stoka) Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which confidential friend of Krsna serves as the general leading the childhood friends during military pastimes?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Bhadrasena is the general who leads Krsna's childhood friends in military pastimes.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of Krsna's confidential friends is described as being just like a small version of Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explains that Stokakrsna is very appropriately named, as he is just like a small, or stoka, Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific meaning associated with the name of Krsna's friend, Stokakrsna?
questions.explanation: The verse notes that Stokakrsna is aptly named because he is just like a small (stoka) Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what role does Bhadrasena fulfill in the playful activities of Krsna's childhood friends?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Bhadrasena as the general who leads the childhood friends in military pastimes.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the nature of Stokakrsna characterized in relation to the Supreme Lord?
options: He is the elder brother of Krsna
He serves as Krsna's personal charioteer
questions.explanation: The text states that Stokakrsna is very appropriately named because he is just like a small (stoka) Krsna.

2.33.verse_text: These confidential friends (priya-sakha) delights Lord Krsna with their enthusiastic and jubliant wrestling, stick-fighting and other sports.

questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions that the priya-sakha delight Krsna through their enthusiastic and jubilant participation in wrestling and stick-fighting.

options: They cause Him to be anxious
They cause Him to be indifferent
questions.explanation: The verse states that these confidential friends specifically delight Lord Krsna through their enthusiastic and jubilant sports.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the nature of the sports engaged in by the confidential friends of Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text characterizes the sports performed by the priya-sakha as enthusiastic and jubilant.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the confidential friends (priya-sakha) collectively called in the context of their relationship with Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the group of confidential friends directly as priya-sakha.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides wrestling and stick-fighting, what other activities are described as sports of the priya-sakha?
questions.explanation: The text mentions wrestling and stick-fighting, followed by the phrase 'and other sports'.

2.34.verse_text: These confidential friends are all very peaceful by nature. Each of them considers Lord Krsna equal to his own life's breath.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the inherent nature described of Krsna's confidential friends?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that these confidential friends are all very peaceful by nature.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how do Krsna's confidential friends regard the Lord in relation to their own existence?
options: As equal to their own life's breath
questions.correct_answer: As equal to their own life's breath
questions.explanation: The text states that each of these confidential friends considers Lord Krsna equal to his own life's breath.

2.35.verse_text: Krsna's Intimate Friends (priya-narma-sakha): Subala, Arjuna, Gandharva, Vasanta, Ujjvala, Kokila, Sanandana and Vidagdha are the msot important of Krsna's intimate friends.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which cowherd boy is specifically listed among the most important of Krsna's intimate friends, the priya-narma-sakhas?

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many names are provided as the most important of Krsna's intimate friends in this specific list?

questions.explanation: Vidagdha is listed by name in the text as one of the most important priya-narma-sakhas.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, is Vasanta mentioned as one of the most important of Krsna's intimate friends?

No, he is a joking friend

2.36.verse_text: Krsna keeps no secrets from these intimate friends. Among them Madhumangala, Puspanka and Hasanka are the leaders of those fond of joking. Handsome Sanandana is very pleased by his close friendship with Krsna and splendid Ujjvala appears like the personified ruler of all transcendental mellows. Lord Krsna, the crest jewel of playful boys, is submissive to His dear friend Ujjvala.

questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that Madhumangala, Puspanka, and Hasanka are the leaders of those intimate friends who are fond of joking.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which intimate friend of Krsna is described as appearing like the personified ruler of all transcendental mellows?
questions.explanation: The text describes the handsome Ujjvala as appearing like the personified ruler of all transcendental mellows.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, to which dear friend is Lord Krsna, the crest jewel of playful boys, submissive?
questions.explanation: The verse concludes by stating that Lord Krsna is submissive to His dear friend Ujjvala.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is said regarding the intimate friends and the secrets of Krsna?
options: Krsna keeps no secrets from them
Krsna reveals only minor secrets to them
Krsna shares secrets only during the night
Krsna tests them before sharing secrets

questions.explanation: The text clearly states that Krsna keeps no secrets from these intimate friends.

He is a witness to pastimes
He is a great reservoir of the nectar of innumerable transcendental pastimes
He is a creator of all transcendental pastimes

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two divine personalities are identified as the younger sisters of the brother mentioned in this verse?

He is adorned with peacock feathers
He wears a garland of forest flowers

Engaging in wrestling matches with Krishna

questions.explanation: The verse explicitly states that this personality is expert at arranging the meeting of the divine couple.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the internal emotional state of this dear friend of Krishna described?
options: Full of transcendental love for the Divine Couple
Filled with deep concern for the safety of Vraja

Immersed in silent meditation on the lotus feet

He is always absorbed in the contemplation of the forest beauty.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what term identifies the group of devotees who are expert in music, drama, and the science of scents for the pleasure of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies the vitas as the specific group of devotees who possess these artistic expertises.

2.74-75.verse_text: Krsna's Servants: Bhangura, Brngara, Sandhika, Grahila, Raktaka, Patraka, Patri, Madhukantha, Madhuvrata, Salika, Talika, Mali, Mana and Maladhara are the most prominent of Lord Krsna's servants.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is listed as one of the most prominent servants of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Bhangura as one of the prominent servants of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following groups of individuals are identified as the most prominent servants of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse lists Brngara, Sandhika, and Grahila among the primary servants serving Lord Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which servant is mentioned alongside Madhukantha and Madhuvrata in the list of Krsna's prominent attendants?
questions.explanation: Salika is identified in the specific list of Krsna's prominent servants appearing in the verses.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is named as a prominent servant of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: Patraka is clearly cited in the text as one of the prominent servants of the Lord.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these names is included in the enumeration of Lord Krsna's most prominent servants?
questions.explanation: The text lists Maladhara among the prominent servants who attend to Lord Krsna.

2.76.verse_text: These servants carry Krsna's venu and murali flutes, buffalo-horn bugle, stick, rope and other paraphernalia. They also bring the mineral dyes (the cowherd boys use to decorate their bodies).

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the primary musical instruments carried by the servants of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that these servants are responsible for carrying Krsna's venu and murali flutes.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides flutes, what signaling instrument is listed among the paraphernalia carried by Krsna's servants?
questions.explanation: The text mentions the buffalo-horn bugle as one of the specific items carried by the servants of the Lord.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what do the servants of Lord Krsna bring to assist the cowherd boys in decorating their bodies?
questions.explanation: The text notes that these servants bring the mineral dyes used by the cowherd boys for body decoration.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following items is mentioned as being carried by Krsna's servants along with His stick and rope?
questions.explanation: The text lists the stick, rope, and murali flute as the specific paraphernalia carried by these attendants.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the nature of the service performed by the assistants who carry the flute and bugle?
options: They carry paraphernalia and body-decorating dyes
They prepare the Lord's midday feast
They clean the grazing pastures for the cows
They guard the forest paths at night
questions.correct_answer: They carry paraphernalia and body-decorating dyes
questions.explanation: The text summarizes their service as carrying the Lord's flute, bugle, stick, rope, and the dyes used for body decoration.

2.77-78.verse_text: Betel-nut Servants: Pallva, Mangala, Phulla, Komala, Kapila, Suvilasa, Vilasaksa, Rasala, Rasasali and Jambula are the most important of Lord Krsna's betel-nut servants. They are younger than Krsna and always expert at singing and playing musical instruments. They are younger than Krsna and always stay near Him.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these young cowherd boys is explicitly identified as one of the most important servants responsible for preparing and serving betel-nut to Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: Pallva is explicitly named in the list of the most important servants engaged in the service of providing betel-nut to Lord Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how does the scripture describe the relative age of the servants like Jambula and Rasala in relation to Lord Krsna?
options: They are the same age as Krsna
They are much older than Balarama
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that these betel-nut servants are younger than Krsna and always stay near Him.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides their primary service of offering betel-nut, what other talents are the servants such as Mangala, Phulla, and Suvilasa noted for?
options: Expertise in singing and playing musical instruments
Skill in wrestling and hand-to-hand combat
Talent in training the forest birds
Ability to weave flower garlands for the Divine Couple
questions.correct_answer: Expertise in singing and playing musical instruments
questions.explanation: The verses specify that these servants are always expert at singing and playing musical instruments in addition to their service.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which set of names belongs to the group of servants who serve Lord Krsna by providing betel-nut?
questions.explanation: Kapila, Suvilasa, Vilasaksa, and Rasala are all listed among the primary servants designated for betel-nut service.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the habitual proximity of the betel-nut servants like Komala and Rasasali to Lord Krsna?
options: They only approach Him during the evening
They guard the perimeter of the Govardhana hill
They remain at a distance out of reverence
questions.explanation: The scripture clearly states that these betel-nut servants are younger than Krsna and always stay near Him.

2.79.verse_text: Water Carriers: Payoda and Varida are the most important of the servants engaged in carrying water for Lord Krsna. Clothing Washers: Saranga and Bakula are the most important of the servants engaged in expertly washing Lord Krsna's laundry.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which two devotees serve Lord Krsna specifically by carrying water for Him?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Payoda and Varida as the most important servants engaged in carrying water for Lord Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the two primary servants responsible for expertly washing the laundry of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly states that Saranga and Bakula are the most important servants engaged in expertly washing Lord Krsna's laundry.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specialized service rendered to Lord Krsna by the devotees named Saranga and Bakula?
questions.explanation: The scripture identifies Saranga and Bakula as the servants who expertly wash the laundry of Lord Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what specific duty is performed by the servants known as Payoda and Varida?
questions.explanation: The text defines Payoda and Varida as the foremost servants in the service of carrying water for Lord Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, among the personal servants of Lord Krsna, which of these pairs is tasked with the washing of His garments?
questions.explanation: Saranga and Bakula are cited by the author as the most important of the servants who expertly wash Lord Krsna's laundry.

2.80.verse_text: Decorators: Premakanda, Mahagandha, Sairindhra, Madhu, Kandala and Makaranda are the most important of the servants constantly engaged in decorating Lord Krsna with various ornaments and clothing.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which six servants are recognized as the primary decorators responsible for adorning Lord Krishna with ornaments and clothing?
options: Premakanda, Mahagandha, Sairindhra, Madhu, Kandala, and Makaranda
Payoda, Varida, Saranga, Bakula, Sumanah, and Kusumollasa
Sumanah, Kusumollasa, Puspahara, Hara, Madhu, and Kandala
Sairindhra, Makaranda, Payoda, Varida, Saranga, and Bakula
questions.correct_answer: Premakanda, Mahagandha, Sairindhra, Madhu, Kandala, and Makaranda
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly lists Premakanda, Mahagandha, Sairindhra, Madhu, Kandala, and Makaranda as the principal servants who decorate Lord Krishna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the specific primary duty performed by the group of servants led by Premakanda and Mahagandha?
options: Decorating Lord Krishna with ornaments and clothing
Carrying water for Lord Krishna's use
questions.correct_answer: Decorating Lord Krishna with ornaments and clothing
questions.explanation: The text identifies Premakanda and his associates as those constantly engaged in the service of decorating Lord Krishna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following is identified as one of the six primary decorators of Lord Krishna?
questions.explanation: Sairindhra is listed in the verse as one of the six most important servants engaged in decorating Lord Krishna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which servant listed among the decorators shares a name with a famous associate of Krishna?
questions.explanation: The name Madhu is explicitly included among the six servants listed in the verse who are responsible for decorating the Lord.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many servants are specifically mentioned as the most important in the role of decorating Lord Krishna?
questions.explanation: The verse names exactly six servants: Premakanda, Mahagandha, Sairindhra, Madhu, Kandala, and Makaranda.

2.81.verse_text: Servants Who Provide Aromatic Substances: Sumanah, Kusumollasa, Puspahara, Hara and others expertly provide Krsna with various aromatic substances such as flowers, flower ornaments, flower garlands and camphor.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which servants are specifically highlighted for their expert service in providing Krsna with aromatic substances like camphor and floral items?
questions.explanation: The verse explicitly names Sumanah, Kusumollasa, Puspahara, and Hara as the servants tasked with providing aromatic substances and floral items to the Lord.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific aromatic substance is mentioned as being provided by Sumanah and his companions to the Lord?
questions.explanation: The text identifies flowers, flower ornaments, flower garlands, and camphor as the specific aromatic items provided by the group led by Sumanah.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides camphor, what are the primary decorative items provided by the servants Sumanah, Kusumollasa, Puspahara, and Hara?
options: Flowers, flower ornaments, and flower garlands
Royal crowns, ear-rings, and jewel necklaces
questions.correct_answer: Flowers, flower ornaments, and flower garlands
questions.explanation: The scripture identifies these servants as providing various aromatic substances specifically in the form of flowers, flower ornaments, and flower garlands.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how is the nature of the service provided by Sumanah and his associates described?
options: As being done with great hesitance
As being done only during the nighttime
As being done through divine inspiration
questions.explanation: The verse describes Sumanah and his group as expertly providing Krsna with various aromatic substances.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the collective role assigned to the servants Sumanah, Kusumollasa, Puspahara, and Hara?
options: They are the decorators of the Lord's bodily ornaments
They are the providers of aromatic substances
They are the managers of the Lord's kitchen
They are the guardians of the Lord's treasury
questions.correct_answer: They are the providers of aromatic substances
questions.explanation: The verse identifies these specific individuals as the servants who provide aromatic substances to the Lord.

2.82.verse_text: The Napitas: Svaccha, Susila, Praguna and others are engaged in various services, such as caring for the Lord's hair, massaging Him, giving Him a mirror and guarding over His treasury. Others: Vimala, Komala and others are engaged in various services, such as caring for the Lord's kitchen.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which individuals are specifically identified as the napitas who provide services such as massaging the Lord and caring for His hair?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Svaccha, Susila, and Praguna as the napitas engaged in caring for the Lord's hair and massaging Him.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific service is mentioned alongside caring for the Lord's hair and massaging Him?
questions.explanation: The text states that the napitas are engaged in services including caring for the Lord's hair, massaging Him, giving Him a mirror, and guarding over His treasury.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which associates are specifically described as being engaged in the service of the Lord's kitchen?
questions.explanation: The text identifies Vimala, Komala, and others as those engaged in various services, specifically caring for the Lord's kitchen.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, besides massaging the Lord and caring for His hair, what other personal item are the napitas noted for providing to the Lord?
questions.explanation: The verse states the napitas are engaged in services such as caring for the Lord's hair, massaging Him, and giving Him a mirror.

2.83.verse_text: Maidservants: Dhanistha-devi, Candanakala-devi, Gunamala-devi, Ratiprabha-devi, Taruni-devi, Induprabha-devi, Sobha-devi and Rambha-devi are the leaders of the gopis engaged in Krsna's service. These gopis are expert at cleaning and decorating Krsna's home, anointing it with various aromatic substances, carrying milk and performing other duties.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which group of gopis serves as the leaders engaged in the direct service of Lord Krsna?
options: Dhanistha-devi, Candanakala-devi, Gunamala-devi, Ratiprabha-devi, Taruni-devi, Induprabha-devi, Sobha-devi, and Rambha-devi
Kurangi-devi, Bhrngari-devi, Sulamba-devi, Alambika-devi, and their associates
Svaccha, Susila, Praguna, Vimala, and Komala
Radha, Lalita, Visakha, Citra, Indurekha, and Ranga-devi
questions.correct_answer: Dhanistha-devi, Candanakala-devi, Gunamala-devi, Ratiprabha-devi, Taruni-devi, Induprabha-devi, Sobha-devi, and Rambha-devi
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names these eight gopis as the leaders among those who serve Krsna.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the primary household duties performed by the gopis led by Dhanistha-devi?
options: Composing music, dancing, and playing the flute
Cleaning and decorating Krsna's home, anointing it with aromatic substances, and carrying milk
Massaging the Lord's feet and offering garlands
Guarding the treasury and managing the cowherd camps
questions.correct_answer: Cleaning and decorating Krsna's home, anointing it with aromatic substances, and carrying milk
questions.explanation: The verse specifies that these gopis are expert in cleaning, decorating, anointing the home with aromatic substances, and carrying milk.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which specific service involves applying fragrant substances within the residence of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The text identifies anointing the home with aromatic substances as a specific expert duty of the maidservants.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which gopi is mentioned as the first among the leaders listed in the service of Lord Krsna?
questions.explanation: The list of gopis begins with the name Dhanistha-devi, who is identified as a leader.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, in addition to decorating and anointing, what is one of the specific duties performed by the group of gopis led by Dhanistha-devi?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly mentions 'carrying milk' as one of the other duties performed by this group of gopis.

2.84.verse_text: Other Maidservants: Kurangi-devi, Bhrngari-devi, Sulamba-devi, Alambika-devi and other gopis also serve Krsna in these ways.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these gopis is explicitly mentioned as a maidservant who serves Krsna in the same manner as the other leaders of the gopis?
questions.explanation: The text lists Kurangi-devi alongside Bhrngari-devi, Sulamba-devi, and Alambika-devi as maidservants who serve Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of the following gopis is listed in the group of maidservants that includes Bhrngari-devi and Sulamba-devi?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Alambika-devi as one of the maidservants who serves Krsna, grouped with Bhrngari-devi and Sulamba-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which gopi is mentioned as serving Krsna, alongside Kurangi-devi and Alambika-devi?
questions.explanation: Bhrngari-devi is listed in the verse alongside Kurangi-devi and Alambika-devi as one of the maidservants serving Krsna.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which gopi is included in the specific list of maidservants along with Kurangi-devi and Bhrngari-devi?
questions.explanation: Sulamba-devi is one of the maidservants explicitly named in the verse as serving Krsna in the company of Kurangi-devi and Bhrngari-devi.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how are the maidservants such as Kurangi-devi and Bhrngari-devi engaged in their service to Krsna?
options: They serve Krsna in the same ways as the previously mentioned leaders
They act as spies in various disguises
They carry messages exclusively from Radha
They are tasked only with tending the cows in the pasture
questions.correct_answer: They serve Krsna in the same ways as the previously mentioned leaders
questions.explanation: The verse states that Kurangi-devi, Bhrngari-devi, and others serve Krsna in these ways, referring to the duties mentioned in the preceding context.

2.85.verse_text: Spies: Catura, Carana, Dhiman and Pesala are the leaders of Krsna's expert spies, who travel in various disguises among the cowherd men and gopis.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the leaders of the group that serves Krsna as expert spies?
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Catura, Carana, Dhiman, and Pesala as the leaders of Krsna's expert spies.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the primary occupation or specialized service provided by Catura, Carana, Dhiman, and Pesala to Krsna?
questions.explanation: The verse identifies Catura, Carana, Dhiman, and Pesala as leaders of Krsna's expert spies.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how do the leaders of Krsna's spies, such as Catura and Dhiman, operate among the gopas and gopis?
questions.explanation: The text states that these spies travel in various disguises among the cowherd men and gopis.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which individuals are identified as the leaders who navigate Vraja in various disguises to gather information for Krsna?
questions.explanation: Catura, Carana, Dhiman, and Pesala are specifically identified as the spy leaders who employ various disguises.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what is the demographic environment where Krsna's expert spies, led by Catura and his companions, perform their duties?
options: Among the cowherd men and gopis
Within the royal court of Kamsa
Within the forest of Vrindavana alone
questions.correct_answer: Among the cowherd men and gopis
questions.explanation: The verse states the spies travel among the cowherd men (gopa) and the gopis.

2.86.verse_text: Gopa Messengers: Visarada, Tunga, Vavaduka, Manorama and Nitisara are the leaders of the gopa messengers. They carry Krsna's messages to the gopis to arrange for pastimes and also to settle quarrels.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, who are the leaders of the gopa messengers who serve Krishna?
options: Visarada, Tunga, Vavaduka, Manorama and Nitisara
Catura, Carana, Dhiman, Pesala and Tunga
Paurnamasi, Vrnda, Vamsi, Nandimukhi and Mela
Vira-devi, Vrndarika-devi, Murali-devi, Tunga and Visarada
questions.correct_answer: Visarada, Tunga, Vavaduka, Manorama and Nitisara
questions.explanation: The text explicitly names Visarada, Tunga, Vavaduka, Manorama and Nitisara as the leaders of the gopa messengers.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, what are the primary duties performed by the gopa messengers headed by Visarada and Nitisara?
options: Collecting flowers and preparing garlands for the Divine Couple
Carrying messages to the gopis to arrange pastimes and settle quarrels
Spying on the movements of Kamsa's agents in the village of Vraja
Managing the grazing schedules for the cows and calves in the forest
questions.correct_answer: Carrying messages to the gopis to arrange pastimes and settle quarrels
questions.explanation: The text defines their role as carrying messages to the gopis specifically for arranging pastimes and settling quarrels.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, which of these is one of the five leaders of the gopa messengers?
questions.explanation: Vavaduka is listed as one of the five leaders alongside Visarada, Tunga, Manorama, and Nitisara.

questions.question: In Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, how many gopa messengers are identified as leaders?
questions.explanation: The text identifies exactly five individuals: Visarada, Tunga, Vavaduka, Manorama and Nitisara.

questions.question: According to Radha-Krishna-Ganoddesha-Dipika, if there is a quarrel between the Divine Couple and the gopis, who is responsible for carrying the message to help settle it?
options: The gopa messengers such as Manorama
The gopi messengers such as Vrnda-devi
questions.correct_answer: The gopa messengers such as Manorama
questions.explanation: The text specifies that the gopa messengers led by Manorama and others carry messages to settle quarrels.

2.87.verse_text: Krsna's Gopi Messengers: Paurnamasi-devi, Vira-devi, Vrnda-devi, Vamsi-devi, Nandimukhi-devi, Vrndarika-devi, Mela-devi and Murali-devi are the leaders of Krsna's gopi messengers.
